conditions.title: Access and participation plan

criteria.text: An access and participation plan that has been approved by the OfS is in force.
criteria.threshold: Approved access and participation plan must be in force
risk_indicators: Access and participation plan has expired or been withdrawn
Plan has not received formal OfS approval
Institution operating without any current access and participation plan
Significant changes to provision not reflected in approved plan

criteria.text: Access and participation plan
criteria.threshold: Must have an approved access and participation plan
risk_indicators: Institution operating without an approved access and participation plan
Access and participation plan approval has expired or been withdrawn
Institution has failed to submit required access and participation plan updates
OfS has raised concerns about the institution's access and participation plan compliance

criteria.text: Have in force an access and participation plan approved by the OfS in accordance with HERA.
criteria.threshold: Maintain a current OfS-approved access and participation plan
risk_indicators: Access and participation plan approaching expiry date without renewal application
OfS rejection or withdrawal of approval for access and participation plan
Significant changes to course offerings or fee structure without corresponding plan updates
Gaps in plan coverage during transition periods between old and new plans

criteria.text: Take all reasonable steps to comply with the provisions of the plan.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate reasonable efforts to implement all plan provisions
risk_indicators: Consistent failure to meet access and participation targets specified in the plan
Inadequate resource allocation or staffing for plan implementation activities
Poor monitoring and reporting systems for tracking plan compliance
Lack of corrective action when plan provisions are not being met

criteria.text: To register in the Approved (fee cap) category and be able to charge fees above the basic amount to qualifying persons on qualifying courses subject to regulated undergraduate fees during an academic year for which the access and participation plan is in force, a provider must first have this plan approved by the OfS.
criteria.threshold: Must have OfS-approved access and participation plan before charging fees above basic amount
risk_indicators: Charging fees above basic amount without an approved plan
Access and participation plan approval has expired or been withdrawn
Provider operating in Approved (fee cap) category without current plan
Failure to submit plan for approval before fee charging period

criteria.text: A provider will satisfy this condition by continuing to have an approved access and participation plan in force for any period in which it intends to charge fees above the basic amount.
criteria.threshold: Must maintain approved access and participation plan throughout any period of higher fee charging
risk_indicators: Gap between plan expiry and renewal approval
Plan approval revoked during fee charging period
Provider announces intention to charge higher fees without plan in force
Non-compliance with plan conditions leading to potential withdrawal

criteria.text: The progress made by the provider in delivering the objectives and targets in its plan.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrable progress in delivering specific objectives and targets set out in the access and participation plan
risk_indicators: Consistent underperformance against plan targets without remedial action
Lack of documented progress monitoring or reporting mechanisms
Failure to implement planned interventions or activities within agreed timescales
Declining performance metrics in access, success, or progression indicators

criteria.text: The approach and actions the provider has taken to deliver the provisions of its plan.
criteria.threshold: Evidence of systematic approach and concrete actions taken to implement all provisions of the access and participation plan
risk_indicators: Absence of clear implementation strategies or action plans
Inadequate resource allocation to support plan delivery
Governing body lacks engagement with plan monitoring and oversight
No appropriate arrangements in place to monitor performance against plan provisions

criteria.text: Have in force an access and participation plan approved by the OfS in accordance with the Higher Education and Research Act 2017 (HERA).
criteria.threshold: Must have an OfS-approved access and participation plan in force
risk_indicators: Access and participation plan has expired or been withdrawn by OfS
Provider submitting late or incomplete plan renewal applications
OfS communications indicating plan approval is at risk
Changes to fee structure without corresponding plan updates

criteria.text: Take all reasonable steps to comply with the provisions of the plan.
criteria.threshold: Must demonstrate reasonable efforts to comply with all plan provisions
risk_indicators: Significant underperformance against access and participation targets
Failure to implement planned outreach or student support activities
OfS monitoring indicating non-compliance with plan commitments
Evidence of inadequate resource allocation to meet plan objectives

conditions.notes: This condition requires institutions to have an OfS-approved access and participation plan that remains in force. The document references sections on Summary (85), Guidance (85), Assessment (86), and Behaviours (86).

conditions.title: Access and participation statement

criteria.text: The provider must have an access and participation statement that meets the requirements set out in condition A2
criteria.threshold: Must maintain compliant access and participation statement
risk_indicators: Access and participation statement is missing or incomplete
Statement does not address required elements or groups
No evidence of implementation of stated commitments
Failure to update statement within required timeframes

criteria.text: The provider must have in force an access and participation statement that has been approved by the OfS.
criteria.threshold: Must maintain an OfS-approved access and participation statement
risk_indicators: No current access and participation statement on file with OfS
Access and participation statement has expired or been withdrawn
Statement submitted but not yet approved by OfS
Operating without required OfS approval for access arrangements

criteria.text: Publish an access and participation statement.
criteria.threshold: Access and participation statement must be published
risk_indicators: No access and participation statement found on provider's website
Statement exists but is not publicly accessible or easily findable
Statement lacks required content or appears incomplete
Provider has been charging fees without having published the required statement

criteria.text: Update and re-publish this statement on an annual basis.
criteria.threshold: Statement must be updated and re-published annually
risk_indicators: Access and participation statement has not been updated for more than 12 months
Statement shows outdated information or references previous academic years without updates
No evidence of annual review or revision process
Published statement does not reflect current access and participation activities

criteria.text: To register in either of the Approved categories and charge fees up to the basic amount for qualifying undergraduate courses, a provider must publish an access and participation statement.
criteria.threshold: Publication of an access and participation statement
risk_indicators: No access and participation statement published on provider's website
Statement has not been updated for over two years
Statement lacks specific commitments to supporting disadvantaged students
Statement does not reflect the provider's current student demographics or circumstances

criteria.text: A provider will satisfy this condition by updating and republishing its statement each year.
criteria.threshold: Annual update and republication of access and participation statement
risk_indicators: Provider has not updated and republished its statement each year
Statement is not accessible to current and future students and to the wider public
Statement is not published in an easily accessible place on the provider's website
Statement has not been updated during routine monitoring checks

criteria.text: Publish an access and participation statement.
criteria.threshold: Publication of an access and participation statement
risk_indicators: No access and participation statement published on provider website
Access and participation statement is incomplete or lacks required information
Statement has not been made publicly available to prospective students
Provider cannot demonstrate when statement was first published

criteria.text: Update and re-publish this statement on an annual basis.
criteria.threshold: Annual update and re-publication of the access and participation statement
risk_indicators: Statement has not been updated for more than 12 months
Updated statement has not been re-published or made available
No evidence of annual review process for the statement
Failure to communicate updates to relevant stakeholders

conditions.notes: Document references summary, guidance, assessment and behaviours sections at pages 87-88, with quality and standards conditions and general ongoing conditions following at page 89.

conditions.title: Academic experience

criteria.text: The provider must deliver well-designed courses that provide a high-quality academic experience for all students.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate well-designed courses providing high-quality academic experience
risk_indicators: Student complaints about course design or academic quality
Low student satisfaction scores in academic experience metrics
External examiner reports highlighting course design deficiencies
High dropout rates or academic failure rates

criteria.text: Without prejudice to the principles and requirements provided for by any other condition of registration and the scope of B1.1, the provider must ensure that the students registered on each higher education course receive a high quality academic experience.
criteria.threshold: Students receive a high quality academic experience on each course
risk_indicators: Student complaints or surveys indicating poor course quality
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about academic standards
High dropout rates or poor student progression
Failure to update course content or teaching materials regularly

criteria.text: For the purposes of this condition, a high quality academic experience includes but is not limited to ensuring all of the following: a. each higher education course is up-to-date; b. each higher education course provides educational challenge; c. each higher education course is coherent; d. each higher education course is effectively delivered; and e. each higher education course, as appropriate to the subject matter of the course, requires students to develop relevant skills.
criteria.threshold: Courses are up-to-date, challenging, coherent, effectively delivered, and develop relevant skills
risk_indicators: Outdated curriculum not reflecting current industry standards or academic developments
Evidence of courses lacking appropriate academic rigor or challenge
Disjointed course structure or learning outcomes not aligned with content
Poor teaching delivery evidenced through student feedback or observation

criteria.text: "appropriately informed" will be assessed by reference to: i. the time period within which any of the developments described in the definition of up-to-date have been in existence; ii. the importance of any of the developments described in the definition of up-to-date to the subject matter of the higher education course; and iii. the time period by which it is planned that such developments described in the definition of up-to-date will be brought into the higher education course content.
criteria.threshold: Course content must incorporate recent developments in appropriate timeframes based on their importance to the subject matter
risk_indicators: Course materials reference outdated research or practices that are no longer current in the field
Significant time lag between important industry/academic developments and their incorporation into course content
No documented process for regularly reviewing and updating course content with recent developments
Student feedback indicates course content lacks currency compared to industry standards

criteria.text: "coherent" means a higher education course which ensures: i. there is an appropriate balance between breadth and depth of content; ii. subjects and skills are taught in an appropriate order and, where necessary, build on each other throughout the course; and iii. key concepts are introduced at the appropriate point in the course content.
criteria.threshold: Course structure must demonstrate logical progression, appropriate balance of content, and well-sequenced learning
risk_indicators: Students struggle with advanced concepts due to inadequate foundational knowledge from earlier modules
Course content lacks clear learning progression or prerequisite relationships between modules
Excessive breadth of topics without sufficient depth to meet learning outcomes
Key concepts introduced too late or too early in the course sequence

criteria.text: "educational challenge" means a challenge that is no less than the minimum level of rigour and difficulty reasonably expected of the higher education course, in the context of the subject matter and level of the course.
criteria.threshold: Course must maintain appropriate academic rigour and difficulty for its subject and level
risk_indicators: Graduate outcomes significantly below sector benchmarks for similar courses
Assessment tasks consistently completed with minimal effort or time investment
External examiner reports questioning the academic level or rigour of the course
Low student engagement or attendance due to insufficient intellectual challenge

criteria.text: "effectively delivered", in relation to a higher education course, means the manner in which it is taught, supervised and assessed (both in person and remotely) including, but not limited to, ensuring: i. an appropriate balance between delivery methods, for example lectures, seminars, group work or practical study, as relevant to the content of the course; and ii. an appropriate balance between directed and independent study or research, as relevant to the level of the course.
criteria.threshold: Course delivery must use appropriate teaching methods and balance between directed and independent learning
risk_indicators: Over-reliance on single delivery method inappropriate for course content or learning outcomes
Insufficient independent learning opportunities for higher-level courses
Poor student satisfaction scores relating to teaching methods or course organization
Inadequate supervision or support for independent study components

criteria.text: "relevant skills" means: i. knowledge and understanding relevant to the subject matter and level of the higher education course; and ii. other skills relevant to the subject matter and level of the higher education course including, but not limited to, cognitive skills, practical skills, transferable skills and professional competences.
criteria.threshold: Course must develop both subject-specific knowledge and broader skills appropriate to the discipline and level
risk_indicators: Graduate employment outcomes suggest inadequate development of professional competences
Course focuses solely on theoretical knowledge without practical skill development
Missing transferable skills that employers expect from graduates in the field
Assessment methods fail to evaluate the full range of intended learning outcomes

criteria.text: "up-to-date" means representative of current thinking and practices in the subject matter to which the higher education course relates, including being appropriately informed by recent: i. subject matter developments; ii. research, industrial and professional developments; and iii. developments in teaching and learning, including learning resources.
criteria.threshold: Course content must reflect current thinking, research, and professional practice in the field
risk_indicators: Reading lists dominated by publications more than 10 years old in rapidly evolving fields
Course content ignores significant recent developments in research or professional practice
Teaching methods fail to incorporate modern pedagogical approaches and technologies
Industry partnerships or professional body feedback indicates course content is outdated

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All higher education provision regardless of format, level, or delivery method must comply
risk_indicators: Provider excludes certain course types from quality assurance processes
Different standards applied to online versus face-to-face delivery
Microcredentials or apprenticeships treated as exempt from regulatory requirements

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: All students and partnership arrangements must be covered regardless of location or registration status
risk_indicators: Different compliance standards for international partnerships
Non-UK students excluded from quality assurance processes
Partnership arrangements lack proper oversight mechanisms

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it is the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider has any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding bodies must ensure compliance even when not involved in course delivery
risk_indicators: Awarding body delegates responsibility without maintaining oversight
Lack of quality assurance processes for externally delivered courses
Insufficient monitoring of courses leading to provider's awards

criteria.text: Where a provider is not the awarding body for a course, this condition applies to a course the provider itself delivers, or which is delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, this means for example, that a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by Pearson is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses. Similarly, a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by another higher education provider, whether that awarding provider is located in England or elsewhere, is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Delivering providers must ensure compliance regardless of awarding body identity or registration status
risk_indicators: Provider assumes external awarding body handles all compliance requirements
Lack of quality controls for courses with non-registered awarding bodies
Different standards applied based on awarding body type or location

criteria.text: The subject matter of this condition includes matters that relate to the curriculum and pedagogy for higher education courses. The OfS would expect to draw on expert academic judgement, including from subject experts, before reaching a view that the condition was not satisfied in relation to such matters.
criteria.threshold: Curriculum and pedagogy matters must be assessed using expert academic judgement including subject experts
risk_indicators: OfS making judgements on curriculum matters without consulting academic experts
Decisions on pedagogical compliance made solely by non-academic regulators
Subject-specific concerns raised without input from relevant discipline experts
Academic quality assessments conducted without appropriate subject matter expertise

criteria.text: The subject matter of a course must be representative of current thinking and practices, with course content, including topics and reading lists, that is informed by research and scholarship, and reflects professional developments, such as the adoption of the latest professional or industry standards.
criteria.threshold: Course content must be up-to-date and informed by current research, scholarship, and professional standards
risk_indicators: Course content, topics, or reading lists that have not been updated for several years
Curriculum that does not reflect latest professional or industry standards
Teaching materials that are not informed by current research and scholarship
Professional, statutory or regulatory body (PSRB) requirements not met in course content

criteria.text: The pedagogy of a course must be representative of current thinking and practices, including appropriate pedagogy for digital delivery where courses are delivered wholly or in part online.
criteria.threshold: Teaching methods must reflect current pedagogical best practices appropriate to delivery mode
risk_indicators: Online courses using inappropriate pedagogy for digital delivery
Teaching methods that do not reflect current pedagogical thinking
Failure to adapt delivery methods to course requirements
Outdated or ineffective teaching approaches

criteria.text: Courses must provide appropriate educational challenge, with sufficient rigour and difficulty appropriate to the level of study, including integrated masters' courses with appropriately challenging final years and foundation years that prepare students for successful undergraduate study.
criteria.threshold: Educational challenge must be appropriate to the level and type of qualification
risk_indicators: Integrated masters' final year lacking sufficient rigour for the level
Foundation years not preparing students adequately for undergraduate study
Research degree topics providing limited scope for original ideas
Course difficulty not matching the qualification level

criteria.text: Course content must be coherent, providing appropriate breadth and depth, with students secure in foundational topics before progressing, practical components taught in appropriate order, skills taught at the right time, and module choices enabling coherent learning pathways.
criteria.threshold: Course structure must ensure logical progression and coherent learning experience
risk_indicators: Course content too narrow without appropriate optional subjects
Modules containing too much material for appropriate depth of understanding
Students not learning foundational concepts before advanced topics
Practical sessions disconnected from related theory
Module choices not resulting in coherent learning pathways

criteria.text: Courses must be effectively delivered with appropriate range of learning activities, effective delivery methods, supportive research environments, integrated professional elements, and assessment designed to consolidate learning with sufficient and timely feedback.
criteria.threshold: Course delivery must employ effective teaching methods and appropriate learning activities
risk_indicators: Predominantly large-scale lectures without small group teaching opportunities
Insufficient opportunities for direct student-staff engagement
Research courses lacking regular supervision or structured researcher engagement
Professional elements not integrated with academic components
Assessment not scheduled appropriately or feedback insufficient/untimely

criteria.text: Courses must enable students to develop and demonstrate relevant skills, including intellectual skills such as evaluating evidence, mobilising arguments, and solving problems consistent with the subject and level, and professional skills necessary for success in relevant professions where applicable.
criteria.threshold: Students must develop intellectual and professional skills appropriate to their course and career destination
risk_indicators: Courses not requiring development of intellectual skills appropriate to subject and level
Professional courses not developing skills necessary for relevant profession
Absence of skills required for PSRB accreditation where applicable
Skills development not aligned with course level and subject requirements

criteria.text: In order to successfully rely on the exception in B1.4, the nature of the evidence a provider would need to put forward would go beyond articulating potential legal concerns or matters to which it has had regard in its decision-making and would require compelling evidence and reasoning on matters of law.
criteria.threshold: Compelling evidence and reasoning on matters of law demonstrating conflict with Equality Act 2010
risk_indicators: Provider cites general legal concerns without specific evidence of discrimination
Vague or non-specific justifications for course requirement exceptions
Failure to provide legal reasoning tied to particular courses or requirements
Blanket application of exceptions without case-by-case assessment

criteria.text: As the exception in B1.4 only applies 'to the extent' that a provider can demonstrate a conflict with the Equality Act 2010, it would not be sufficient for a provider to put forward evidence and reasoning about its requirements (or absence of requirements) in general terms; rather, a provider would need to address the particular requirements (or absence of requirements) which it is seeking to justify under the exception, and the particular courses to which these relate.
criteria.threshold: Specific evidence addressing particular requirements and particular courses where exception is claimed
risk_indicators: General statements about requirements without course-specific detail
Failure to identify specific requirements being justified under the exception
Broad application of exceptions across multiple courses without individual justification
Lack of particularised evidence for each claimed exception

criteria.text: To give an example, citing this exception, a provider could seek to demonstrate that it is obliged, in order to avoid discrimination under the Equality Act 2010, to design its course requirements in a manner which makes certain allowances for students with dyslexia or other learning disabilities on some courses. If this was the case, the OfS would expect the provider to demonstrate, in the context of the particular courses at issue, that any allowances made were strictly necessary to avoid discrimination under the Equality Act 2010, with reference to compelling evidence and reasoning which supports this.
criteria.threshold: Demonstration that allowances are strictly necessary to avoid discrimination with compelling supporting evidence
risk_indicators: Allowances made without clear necessity to avoid discrimination
Insufficient evidence supporting the need for specific accommodations
Failure to demonstrate strict necessity of course design modifications
Lack of compelling reasoning linking allowances to discrimination avoidance

criteria.text: The provider must ensure that the students registered on each higher education course receive a high quality academic experience.
criteria.threshold: High quality academic experience delivered to all students on each higher education course
risk_indicators: Declining student satisfaction scores in academic experience metrics
Inconsistent quality of teaching or learning resources across courses
Student complaints about inadequate academic support or facilities
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about academic standards

conditions.notes: This condition focuses on ensuring providers deliver courses that are well-designed and provide a high-quality academic experience for all students. The condition includes guidance on information gathering, assessment of evidence and enforcement procedures.

conditions.title: Resources, support and student engagement

criteria.text: The provider has sufficient appropriately qualified staff and sufficient resources to deliver a high-quality academic experience to all students.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate adequate staffing levels and resources for quality delivery
risk_indicators: High staff turnover or vacancy rates in key academic positions
Student complaints about lack of access to essential resources or facilities
Declining student satisfaction scores regarding teaching quality or support
Evidence of overloaded staff unable to provide adequate student supervision

criteria.text: The provider has in place effective arrangements to engage with and provide support to students to enable student success.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate effective student engagement and support systems that enable success
risk_indicators: Low student retention or completion rates
Poor student engagement metrics or attendance patterns
Inadequate academic or pastoral support services
Students reporting lack of access to necessary support when needed

criteria.text: This condition applies to the quality of higher education provided in any manner or form by, or on behalf of, a provider (including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider).
criteria.threshold: Applies to all forms of higher education provision
risk_indicators: Unclear scope of provision covered by quality assurance processes
Gaps in oversight of partner or validation arrangements
Inconsistent application of standards across different modes of delivery

criteria.text: each cohort of students registered on each higher education course receives resources and support which are sufficient for the purpose of ensuring: i. a high quality academic experience for those students; and ii. those students succeed in and beyond higher education
criteria.threshold: Sufficient resources and support for high quality academic experience and student success
risk_indicators: Student complaints about inadequate library, IT or learning resources
Poor student satisfaction scores regarding academic support
High dropout rates or poor progression statistics
Staff-to-student ratios significantly below sector benchmarks

criteria.text: effective engagement with each cohort of students which is sufficient for the purpose of ensuring: i. a high quality academic experience for those students; and ii. those students succeed in and beyond higher education
criteria.threshold: Effective student engagement sufficient for quality experience and success
risk_indicators: Low student engagement in feedback processes or course evaluation
Poor attendance rates or limited participation in academic activities
Minimal student representation in governance structures
Weak mechanisms for capturing and acting on student voice

criteria.text: For the purposes of this condition, "all reasonable steps" is to be interpreted in a manner which (without prejudice to other relevant considerations): a. focuses and places significant weight on: i. the particular academic needs of each cohort of students based on prior academic attainment and capability; and ii. the principle that the greater the academic needs of the cohort of students, the number and nature of the steps needed to be taken are likely to be more significant; b. places less weight, as compared to the factor described in B2.3a., on the provider's financial constraints; and c. disregards case law relating to the interpretation of contractual obligations.
criteria.threshold: Reasonable steps prioritizing student academic needs over financial constraints
risk_indicators: Resource allocation decisions driven primarily by cost rather than student needs
Inadequate support for students with higher academic needs or disadvantaged backgrounds
Evidence of cutting essential services due to financial pressures
Failure to differentiate support based on student cohort characteristics

criteria.text: "academic misconduct" means any action or attempted action that may result in a student obtaining an unfair academic advantage in relation to an assessment, including but not limited to plagiarism, unauthorised collaboration and the possession of unauthorised materials during an assessment.
criteria.threshold: Clear definition and understanding of academic misconduct must be established
risk_indicators: Inconsistent application of academic misconduct policies across departments
High levels of undetected plagiarism or unauthorised collaboration
Students report unclear guidance on what constitutes academic misconduct
Staff lack training on identifying and addressing academic misconduct

criteria.text: "appropriately qualified" means staff have and maintain: i. expert knowledge of the subject they design and/or deliver; ii. teaching qualifications or training, and teaching experience, appropriate for the content and level of the relevant higher education course; and iii. the required knowledge and skills as to the effective delivery of their higher education course.
criteria.threshold: Staff must possess and maintain expert subject knowledge, appropriate teaching qualifications/training/experience, and effective delivery skills
risk_indicators: High staff turnover or difficulty recruiting qualified teaching staff
Teaching staff without relevant subject expertise or qualifications
Lack of professional development opportunities for teaching staff
Student feedback indicating poor quality teaching or outdated content

criteria.text: "assessment" means any component of a course used to assess student achievement towards a relevant award, including an examination and a test.
criteria.threshold: Clear definition of assessment components must be established
risk_indicators: Inconsistent assessment practices across courses or departments
Assessment methods not aligned with learning outcomes
Lack of clear assessment criteria or marking schemes
Student complaints about unfair or unclear assessments

criteria.text: "cohort of students" means the group of students registered on to the higher education course in question and is to be interpreted by reference to the particular academic needs of those students based on prior academic attainment and capability.
criteria.threshold: Student cohorts must be understood and managed based on their particular academic needs and prior attainment
risk_indicators: Poor progression or completion rates within specific cohorts
Failure to adapt teaching to cohort needs and prior attainment levels
Inadequate entry requirements or admissions processes
Students struggling due to mismatched course content and ability levels

criteria.text: "engagement" means routine provision of opportunities for students to contribute to the development of their academic experience and their higher education course, in a way that maintains the academic rigour of that course, including, but not limited to, through membership of the provider's committees, opportunities to provide survey responses, and participation in activities to develop the course and the way it is delivered.
criteria.threshold: Routine opportunities for meaningful student engagement in course development must be provided while maintaining academic rigour
risk_indicators: Low student participation in feedback surveys or committees
Limited or tokenistic student representation in decision-making processes
Student feedback not acted upon or incorporated into course improvements
Lack of formal mechanisms for student input on course development

criteria.text: "higher education course" is to be interpreted: i. in accordance with the Higher Education and Research Act 2017; and ii. so as to include, for the avoidance of doubt: A. a course of study; B. a programme of research; C. any further education course that forms an integrated part of a higher education course; and D. any module that forms part of a higher education course, whether or not that module is delivered as an integrated part of the course.
criteria.threshold: Clear understanding and application of higher education course definition in accordance with HERA 2017
risk_indicators: Confusion about which courses fall under higher education regulation
Inconsistent treatment of integrated further education components
Modules not properly integrated or quality assured as part of wider courses
Failure to apply appropriate standards to research programmes

criteria.text: "physical and digital learning resources" includes, as appropriate to the content and delivery of the higher education course, but is not limited to: i. physical locations, for example teaching rooms, libraries, studios and laboratories; ii. physical and digital learning resources, for example books, computers and software; iii. the resources needed for digital learning and teaching, for example, hardware and software, and technical infrastructure; and iv. other specialist resources, for example specialist equipment, software and research tools.
criteria.threshold: Adequate physical and digital learning resources appropriate to course content and delivery must be provided
risk_indicators: Student complaints about inadequate facilities or outdated equipment
IT systems frequently failing or lacking essential software
Library resources insufficient for student numbers or course requirements
Specialist equipment unavailable when needed for teaching or research

criteria.text: "relevant award" means: i. a research award; ii. a taught award; and/or iii. any other type of award or qualification in respect of a higher education course, including an award of credit granted in respect of a module that may form part of a larger higher education course, whether or not granted pursuant to an authorisation given by or under the Higher Education and Research Act 2017, another Act of Parliament or Royal Charter.
criteria.threshold: Clear definition and understanding of relevant awards must be established
risk_indicators: Awards not properly aligned with course content or learning outcomes
Inconsistent credit allocation across modules or courses
Qualification frameworks not properly understood or applied
Awards granted without appropriate academic standards being met

criteria.text: "resources" includes but is not limited to: i. the staff team that designs and delivers a higher education course being collectively sufficient in number, appropriately qualified and deployed effectively to deliver in practice; and ii. physical and digital learning resources that are adequate and deployed effectively to meet the needs of the cohort of students.
criteria.threshold: Sufficient, appropriately qualified staff and adequate physical/digital resources must be effectively deployed
risk_indicators: High student-to-staff ratios affecting quality of education
Courses cancelled or delayed due to insufficient staffing
Resources not effectively deployed to meet student needs
Decline in student satisfaction with resource availability

criteria.text: "sufficient in number" will be assessed by reference to the principle that the larger the cohort size of students, the greater the number of staff and amount of staff time should be available to students, and means, in the context of the staff team: i. there is sufficient financial resource to recruit and retain sufficient staff; ii. the provider allocates appropriate financial resource to ensuring staff are equipped to teach courses; iii. higher education courses have an adequate number of staff, and amount of staff time; and iv. the impact on students of changes in staffing is minimal.
criteria.threshold: Staff numbers must scale appropriately with cohort size and be supported by adequate financial resources
risk_indicators: Staff workloads increasing without corresponding increase in staff numbers
Budget cuts leading to reduced staffing levels
Frequent staffing changes disrupting course delivery
Staff unable to provide adequate individual attention to students

criteria.text: "support" means the effective deployment of assistance, as appropriate to the content of the higher education course and the cohort of students, including but not limited to: i. academic support relating to the content of the higher education course; ii. support needed to underpin successful physical and digital learning and teaching; iii. support relating to understanding, avoiding and reporting academic misconduct; and iv. careers support, but for the avoidance of doubt, does not include other categories of non-academic support.
criteria.threshold: Effective academic support must be deployed appropriately for course content and student cohort needs
risk_indicators: Students unable to access timely academic support when needed
High dropout rates due to lack of appropriate support
Support services not tailored to specific course or student needs
Limited availability of careers guidance or academic misconduct support

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All forms of higher education delivery must be covered regardless of format, level, or recognition status
risk_indicators: Provider excludes certain course types from quality assurance processes
Different standards applied to online versus face-to-face delivery
Microcredentials or apprenticeships treated as exempt from regulatory requirements
Inconsistent oversight of postgraduate research provision

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: All students and partnerships must be covered including international arrangements
risk_indicators: Different standards applied to international versus domestic students
Partnership arrangements lack adequate oversight or monitoring
Non-UK delivery excluded from provider's quality systems
Students taught by third parties receive inconsistent provision

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it is the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider has any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding body responsibility applies even when not involved in course design or delivery
risk_indicators: Awarding body lacks oversight of courses it validates
Insufficient due diligence on delivery partners
Award standards not maintained across different delivery providers
Limited monitoring of courses delivered by other providers

criteria.text: Where a provider is not the awarding body for a course, this condition applies to a course the provider itself delivers, or which is delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, this means for example, that a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by Pearson is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses. Similarly, a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by another higher education provider, whether that awarding provider is located in England or elsewhere, is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Delivery responsibility applies regardless of awarding body identity or registration status
risk_indicators: Provider assumes external awarding body ensures compliance
Inadequate oversight of courses with non-HE awarding bodies
Different compliance standards for different awarding partnerships
Responsibility gaps between delivery and awarding providers

criteria.text: A staff team comprised solely of inexperienced teachers is not likely to be appropriately qualified.
criteria.threshold: Staff teams must include experienced teachers with appropriate qualifications
risk_indicators: High proportion of newly qualified or unqualified teaching staff
Absence of senior academic staff with substantial teaching experience
Staff recruitment focused primarily on recent graduates without teaching background
Student complaints about teaching quality or staff preparedness

criteria.text: A staff team with narrow collective expertise in a subject area is not likely to be appropriately qualified if the range of options available to students extend beyond that collective expertise.
criteria.threshold: Staff collective expertise must cover the full range of course content and student options
risk_indicators: Course modules taught by staff outside their area of expertise
Limited specialization coverage compared to advertised course options
Excessive reliance on guest lecturers for core curriculum areas
Student feedback indicating gaps in specialist knowledge

criteria.text: A staff team in which none of the individuals hold a teaching qualification or have been trained to undertake teaching is not likely to be appropriately qualified.
criteria.threshold: Staff teams must include members with teaching qualifications or training
risk_indicators: No staff hold formal teaching qualifications
Absence of professional development in teaching methods
Poor student evaluation scores for teaching effectiveness
High staff turnover in teaching positions

criteria.text: A staff team that is over-reliant on visiting teachers to deliver large or significant elements of a course is not likely to be appropriately qualified.
criteria.threshold: Core course delivery must be provided by permanent or appropriately committed staff
risk_indicators: High percentage of course hours delivered by visiting/temporary staff
Key modules consistently taught by non-permanent staff
Lack of continuity in course delivery year-on-year
Student complaints about inconsistent teaching standards

criteria.text: A staff team that does not conduct research at the forefront of relevant discipline(s) is not likely to be appropriately qualified to provide supervision to research students.
criteria.threshold: Research supervision requires staff actively engaged in current disciplinary research
risk_indicators: Limited recent research publications by supervisory staff
Absence of active research grants or projects
Supervisors not engaged with current disciplinary developments
Research student complaints about supervision quality or currency

criteria.text: each cohort of students registered on each higher education course receives resources and support which are sufficient for the purpose of ensuring: i. a high quality academic experience for those students; and ii. those students succeed in and beyond higher education
criteria.threshold: Provision of sufficient resources and support for high quality academic experience and student success
risk_indicators: Student complaints about inadequate learning resources or facilities
Declining student satisfaction scores in areas of academic support
High dropout rates or poor progression statistics for specific cohorts
Staff reports of insufficient resources to deliver course requirements

criteria.text: effective engagement with each cohort of students which is sufficient for the purpose of ensuring: i. a high quality academic experience for those students; and ii. those students succeed in and beyond higher education
criteria.threshold: Effective engagement with all student cohorts for high quality experience and success outcomes
risk_indicators: Low response rates to student surveys or feedback mechanisms
Evidence of poor communication between staff and students
Student representatives reporting lack of meaningful consultation
Disparities in engagement levels across different student cohorts

conditions.notes: This condition encompasses the provider's responsibility to maintain adequate resources and staff to deliver quality education, while also establishing effective systems for student engagement and support that enable academic success.

conditions.title: Student outcomes

criteria.text: The provider must deliver successful outcomes for all of its students, which are recognised by employers, industry, esteemed scholarship and/or further study.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate successful outcomes for all students recognised by relevant stakeholders
risk_indicators: Low graduate employment rates or progression to further study
Poor employer feedback on graduate preparedness
Declining student achievement or completion rates
Lack of industry recognition or accreditation for programmes

criteria.text: condition B3, see paragraph 643 of the 'Consultation on a new approach to regulating student outcomes: Analysis of responses to consultation and decisions' document
criteria.threshold: Compliance with student outcomes requirements as specified in consultation document paragraph 643
risk_indicators: Student completion rates declining below sector benchmarks
Graduate employment outcomes falling significantly below expected levels
Student satisfaction scores showing sustained deterioration
Failure to demonstrate improvements in response to student outcome concerns

criteria.text: Without prejudice to the principles and requirements provided for by any other condition of registration and the scope of B3.1, the provider must deliver positive outcomes for students on its higher education courses.
criteria.threshold: Must deliver positive outcomes for students on higher education courses
risk_indicators: Outcome data consistently below OfS numerical thresholds across multiple indicators
High rates of student non-continuation or non-completion without credible justification
Poor graduate employment or progression rates compared to sector benchmarks
Lack of credible action plans or insufficient resource investment to address underperformance

criteria.text: For the purposes of this condition, delivering positive outcomes means that either: a. in the OfS's judgement, the outcome data for each of the indicators and split indicators are at or above the relevant numerical thresholds; or b. to the extent that the provider does not have outcome data for each of the indicators and split indicators that are at or above the relevant numerical thresholds, the OfS otherwise judges that: i. the provider's context justifies the outcome data; and/or ii. this is because the OfS does not hold any data showing the provider's numerical performance against the indicator or split indicator; and/or iii. this is because the OfS does hold this data but the data refers to fewer than the minimum number of students.
criteria.threshold: Outcome data at or above numerical thresholds, or contextual justification for underperformance accepted by OfS
risk_indicators: Outcome data below thresholds without adequate contextual justification
Failure to provide sufficient context or evidence for underperformance
Persistent gaps in data provision or reporting to OfS
Inability to demonstrate credible improvement plans when performance is below thresholds

criteria.text: This condition applies to higher education provided in any manner or form by, or on behalf of, a provider, including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider.
criteria.threshold: Compliance with student outcomes requirements across all forms of higher education provision
risk_indicators: Poor student outcomes data in areas covered by OfS indicators and split indicators
Failure to monitor outcomes for students across different delivery modes or partnership arrangements
Lack of systems to track and improve outcomes for students studying through transnational education or alternative providers
Inconsistent application of quality assurance processes across different types of course provision

criteria.text: A provider is a 'relevant provider' if, in the judgement of the OfS, data showing the provider's performance against the indicators or split indicators exists, and that data relates to any time during the five-year period preceding the date of the provider's application for registration.
criteria.threshold: Must have demonstrable performance data against OfS indicators within five-year period
risk_indicators: Absence of performance data for any five-year period preceding registration
Gaps in data collection from subcontractual teaching arrangements
Missing data due to corporate restructuring or name changes
Inability to provide historical performance evidence against OfS indicators

criteria.text: All registered providers are subject to
criteria.threshold: Compliance with applicable regulatory requirements
risk_indicators: Provider fails to respond to OfS information requests
Evidence of non-compliance with statutory obligations
Lack of systems to monitor regulatory adherence
Failure to report material changes as required

criteria.text: condition B3 as a general ongoing condition
criteria.threshold: Maintain ongoing compliance with condition B3 requirements
risk_indicators: Failure to meet previously established B3 compliance standards
Absence of monitoring systems for ongoing condition requirements
Evidence of declining performance in areas covered by condition B3
Lack of regular reporting or documentation of compliance status

criteria.text: This paragraph sets out the overall requirement of
criteria.threshold: Meet the overall requirement as specified
risk_indicators: Incomplete or fragmented documentation of requirements
Evidence of non-compliance with specified standards
Lack of systematic approach to meeting regulatory obligations
Failure to demonstrate understanding of requirement scope

criteria.text: a provider must deliver positive outcomes for students on its higher education courses
criteria.threshold: positive outcomes must be delivered for students
risk_indicators: Low student satisfaction scores in national surveys
Poor graduate employment rates or outcomes
High dropout or non-completion rates
Declining academic achievement or progression rates

criteria.text: The OfS will consider a provider to be 'delivering positive outcomes' if it is performing at or above each of the numerical thresholds that the OfS has set in relation to the 'indicators' and 'split indicators'.
criteria.threshold: Performance at or above numerical thresholds for all indicators and split indicators
risk_indicators: Performance data showing results below published numerical thresholds for one or more indicators
Consistent downward trends in student outcome metrics across multiple indicators
Insufficient statistical evidence to demonstrate threshold performance when assessed by OfS
Failure to provide adequate contextual justification for below-threshold performance

criteria.text: Where the OfS cannot be satisfied that a provider is performing at or above a relevant numerical threshold on the basis of the data, the OfS will consider whether the context in which the provider is operating nevertheless justifies the provider's outcomes, in that they nevertheless represent positive outcomes for its students.
criteria.threshold: Contextual justification demonstrating positive outcomes despite below-threshold performance
risk_indicators: Below-threshold performance without credible contextual explanation
Weak evidence base for claims about operating context affecting outcomes
Inability to demonstrate that outcomes represent positive results for the specific student population served
Lack of benchmark comparison data to support contextual arguments

criteria.text: Where the OfS does not hold any data on a provider's performance against an indicator or split indicator, or where the OfS does hold data but it refers to fewer students than the minimum number set in technical documents published by the OfS, the OfS will not assess the provider's performance against that indicator or split indicator and this will not prevent the provider from satisfying the condition.
criteria.threshold: Compliance with data submission requirements or demonstration of insufficient student numbers for assessment
risk_indicators: Consistent failure to submit required data for student outcome indicators
Data quality issues preventing meaningful assessment of performance
Artificial manipulation of student numbers to avoid assessment thresholds
Gaps in data provision without legitimate justification

criteria.text: Any external factors the OfS considers to be outside a provider's control that might reasonably be judged to have affected its past performance. When considering a provider's context, the OfS consider external factors where a provider can evidence the impact of those factors on its outcome data.
criteria.threshold: Evidence demonstrating impact of external factors on outcome data
risk_indicators: Unable to provide evidence of external factor impacts on performance data
Claims of external impacts not consistent with sector-wide patterns
External factor explanations not supported by appropriate documentation
Failure to differentiate between controllable and uncontrollable performance factors

criteria.text: Evidence of particular course or profession attributes that are unique to that provider, or a small group of similar providers, and result in performance consistently below a numerical threshold, but that may otherwise be considered positive.
criteria.threshold: Demonstration that below-threshold performance reflects unique positive attributes rather than poor quality
risk_indicators: Below-threshold performance without evidence of unique course attributes
Graduate outcomes survey responses not supporting claims of skills development
Earnings data not demonstrating above-average performance despite threshold issues
Inability to justify profession-specific performance variations

criteria.text: Evidence that a provider no longer delivers, or no longer plans to deliver, courses that are included in the indicators, and the reasons for ceasing the delivery of such courses.
criteria.threshold: Strategic decision-making with appropriate student support and learning application
risk_indicators: Course closures appearing to evade regulatory action rather than improve quality
Inadequate support provided to students on discontinued courses
New courses launched immediately after closing underperforming ones
Failure to apply lessons learned to remaining course portfolio

criteria.text: Evidence of any actions a provider has already taken to improve its performance in relation to numerical thresholds, and the effectiveness and sustainability of those actions.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrated significant and sustained improvement in performance
risk_indicators: Performance improvement actions showing limited or temporary effectiveness
Inability to demonstrate sustained improvement over recent years
Actions taken only after OfS regulatory interest rather than proactive improvement
Aggregate performance remaining below thresholds without clear improvement trajectory

criteria.text: Evidence of a provider's plans to improve its performance in relation to numerical thresholds, and the credibility and sustainability of those plans.
criteria.threshold: Credible and sustainable improvement plans with sufficient resource commitment
risk_indicators: Improvement plans lacking credible implementation details or timescales
Insufficient resource allocation to support proposed performance improvements
Poor track record of delivering previous improvement initiatives
Plans not addressing root causes of identified performance concerns

criteria.text: Where the OfS cannot be satisfied from a provider's outcome data that its performance is at or above each relevant numerical threshold, the OfS will consider whether there is evidence available to the OfS that the provider's context means that any outcome data that is not at or above a relevant numerical threshold is justified, in that it nevertheless represents positive outcomes for students. The OfS will seek further information about contextual factors from a provider where the OfS considers it appropriate to do so.
criteria.threshold: Performance at or above relevant numerical thresholds, or justified contextual reasons for lower performance that still represents positive outcomes
risk_indicators: Student outcome data consistently below numerical thresholds without clear contextual justification
Unable to provide evidence that lower performance represents positive outcomes for students
Failure to respond adequately when OfS seeks information about contextual factors
Pattern of declining student outcomes across multiple metrics

criteria.text: If, after following the step above, the OfS is not satisfied that there is context that means a provider's performance is justified, it will inform the provider that it has taken a provisional decision that the provider has not satisfied initial
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate that contextual factors justify performance below numerical thresholds
risk_indicators: OfS issuing provisional decisions of non-satisfaction
Insufficient evidence provided to justify performance below thresholds
Context provided does not adequately explain poor outcomes
Repeated inability to satisfy OfS following contextual review

criteria.text: The OfS will consider representations from the provider before reaching a final decision.
criteria.threshold: Provider must have opportunity to make representations before final OfS decision
risk_indicators: OfS proceeding to final decision without requesting provider input
Provider representations not being documented or acknowledged
Unreasonably short timeframes for provider responses
OfS failing to provide adequate notice of decision process

criteria.text: Where the OfS does not hold any data on a provider's performance against an indicator or split indicator, the OfS will not assess the provider's performance against that indicator or split indicator and this will not prevent the provider from satisfying the initial condition.
criteria.threshold: No assessment required where OfS holds no performance data
risk_indicators: OfS attempting assessment despite lack of data
Provider being penalised for missing data outside their control
Unreasonable data collection demands from OfS
Assessment proceeding with insufficient data foundation

criteria.text: Where the OfS holds data on a provider's performance against an indicator or split indicator, but that data refers to fewer students than the minimum number set by the OfS in the technical documents, the OfS will not assess the provider's performance against that indicator or split indicator and this will not prevent the provider from satisfying the initial condition.
criteria.threshold: No assessment required where student numbers fall below OfS minimum thresholds
risk_indicators: OfS proceeding with assessment despite insufficient student numbers
Minimum thresholds not clearly defined in technical documents
Provider being assessed on statistically unreliable small cohorts
Inconsistent application of minimum number requirements

criteria.text: Where a provider, or another legal entity that the OfS considers to be operating substantially the same higher education business, has previously been registered, a history of non-compliance with ongoing
criteria.threshold: Previous registration history and compliance record must be considered
risk_indicators: Previous compliance issues being ignored in assessment
Failure to identify connected legal entities with poor compliance history
Inadequate due diligence on provider's corporate structure
Non-compliance patterns not being recognised across related entities

criteria.text: condition B3 is likely to result in a judgement that initial
criteria.threshold: Meet requirements for initial registration judgement
risk_indicators: Incomplete or inadequate documentation submitted for initial assessment
Failure to demonstrate compliance with foundational regulatory requirements
Significant gaps in institutional policies or procedures during initial review
Unable to provide evidence of meeting baseline quality or standards thresholds

criteria.text: The provider must deliver successful outcomes for all of its students, which are recognised by employers, industry, esteemed institutions and others as meeting or exceeding the standards they reasonably expect.
criteria.threshold: Performance must meet or exceed numerical thresholds for student outcome indicators
risk_indicators: Outcome data consistently below relevant numerical thresholds with statistical confidence
High rates of student non-continuation or non-completion
Graduate employment rates significantly below sector averages
Evidence of poor student progression to employment or further study

criteria.text: The provider must deliver positive outcomes for students on its Initial and ongoing higher education courses.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate positive student outcomes across all higher education provision
risk_indicators: Declining completion rates or high dropout rates
Poor graduate employment outcomes or progression rates
Negative student satisfaction scores or feedback trends
Failure to meet sector benchmarks for student outcomes

conditions.notes: This condition requires providers to demonstrate that their students achieve successful outcomes that are valued and recognised in employment, industry, academic circles, or for progression to further study. The condition applies to all students regardless of study mode, level, or subject area.

conditions.title: Assessment and awards

criteria.text: Assessment and awards criteria not explicitly provided in the document excerpt
criteria.threshold: Maintain appropriate assessment standards and award processes
risk_indicators: Inconsistent or unclear assessment criteria across programmes
Unexplained variations in grade distributions or pass rates
Student complaints about unfair or inconsistent marking
External examiner reports highlighting assessment concerns

criteria.text: This condition applies to the quality of higher education provided in any manner or form by, or on behalf of, a provider (including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider).
criteria.threshold: Condition applies to all forms of higher education provision including awards-only arrangements
risk_indicators: Provider unclear about which programmes condition applies to
Inconsistent application across different delivery modes
Confusion about responsibilities in partnership arrangements

criteria.text: students are assessed effectively
criteria.threshold: All students must receive effective assessment
risk_indicators: Students reporting inadequate feedback on assessments
High failure rates without remedial action
Assessment methods not aligned with learning outcomes
Inconsistent assessment practices across programmes

criteria.text: each assessment is valid and reliable
criteria.threshold: All assessments must be both valid and reliable
risk_indicators: Assessment methods not testing intended learning outcomes
Significant variation in marking between assessors
Lack of moderation or second marking processes
Assessment criteria unclear or inconsistently applied

criteria.text: academic regulations are designed to ensure that relevant awards are credible
criteria.threshold: Academic regulations must ensure award credibility
risk_indicators: Academic regulations lacking detail on assessment requirements
Insufficient progression requirements between levels
Weak compensation or condonement rules
Awards granted without meeting published criteria

criteria.text: subject to paragraph B4.3, in respect of each higher education course, academic regulations are designed to ensure the effective assessment of technical proficiency in the English language in a manner which appropriately reflects the level and content of the applicable higher education course
criteria.threshold: Academic regulations must ensure effective assessment of English language proficiency appropriate to course level and content
risk_indicators: No English language assessment requirements in regulations
English language standards inappropriate for course level
Students graduating with inadequate English proficiency
Inconsistent application of English language requirements

criteria.text: relevant awards granted to students are credible at the point of being granted and when compared to those granted previously
criteria.threshold: Awards must be credible both currently and compared to historical standards
risk_indicators: Significant grade inflation over time without justification
Awards granted below expected academic standards
Inconsistency in award standards across cohorts
External examiner concerns about award credibility

criteria.text: The provider must ensure that any higher education course which it provides, and any relevant award granted in respect of such a course, maintain the standards of higher education which may reasonably be expected of such a course or award.
criteria.threshold: Higher education courses and awards maintain expected standards
risk_indicators: Declining academic performance metrics or award classifications over time
Assessment practices that lack rigour or appropriate challenge for the course level
High incidence of academic misconduct or inadequate detection mechanisms
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about standards

criteria.text: Students registered on higher education courses are assessed effectively.
criteria.threshold: Students are assessed in a challenging and appropriately comprehensive way
risk_indicators: Assessment methods that do not provide stretch and rigour consistent with course level
Failure to test relevant skills and knowledge appropriately
Assessment design that facilitates academic misconduct rather than detecting it
Inconsistent or unreliable assessment practices across courses

criteria.text: The standards of relevant awards granted by the provider are credible at the point at which they are granted and when compared over time.
criteria.threshold: Award standards are credible and maintain consistency over time
risk_indicators: Unexplained grade inflation or significant changes in award classifications
Award patterns that differ markedly from comparable providers without justification
Changes to assessment or academic regulations that artificially improve outcomes
Inability to provide convincing evidence for changes in award classifications

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All forms of higher education delivery must be within scope regardless of recognition, level, volume, or delivery method
risk_indicators: Provider excludes certain course types from quality processes claiming they are outside scope
Different quality standards applied to face-to-face versus distance learning delivery
Microcredentials or apprenticeships treated as exempt from condition requirements
Provider fails to monitor quality of courses delivered through mixed delivery approaches

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: Provider responsibility extends to all students and courses regardless of location or partnership arrangements
risk_indicators: Provider treats international partnerships as outside their quality assurance remit
Different standards applied to UK-based versus non-UK-based students
Gaps in oversight of courses delivered through third-party arrangements
Provider claims limited responsibility for students registered but taught elsewhere

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it is the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider has any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding body responsibility for condition compliance applies regardless of involvement in course design or delivery
risk_indicators: Awarding body claims exemption from condition due to non-involvement in teaching
Quality assurance gaps for courses where provider only grants awards
Awarding body fails to monitor standards of courses delivered by others
Provider distances itself from responsibility for partner-delivered courses

criteria.text: Where a provider is not the awarding body for a course, this condition applies to a course the provider itself delivers, or which is delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, this means for example, that a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by Pearson is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses. Similarly, a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by another higher education provider, whether that awarding provider is located in England or elsewhere, is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Teaching provider responsibility for condition compliance applies regardless of awarding body identity or registration status
risk_indicators: Provider claims exemption from condition when delivering courses for external awarding bodies
Quality standards vary depending on whether awarding body is OfS-registered
Provider treats Pearson-awarded courses as outside condition scope
Gaps in compliance for courses with international awarding bodies

criteria.text: In practice, these provisions may result in more than one registered provider being responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to the same course.
criteria.threshold: Multiple provider responsibility for same course compliance is acceptable and expected
risk_indicators: Providers assume other parties have primary responsibility and reduce their oversight
Confusion over accountability leads to gaps in condition compliance
Disputes between providers over who is responsible for meeting condition requirements
Multiple providers fail to coordinate their compliance activities effectively

criteria.text: The provider must ensure that students are assessed effectively and that qualifications awarded are valid, reliable and credible
criteria.threshold: Students assessed effectively with valid, reliable and credible qualifications
risk_indicators: Students at different course stages assessed identically without differentiated marking criteria
Limited range of subject matter or skills assessed on courses
Absence of feedback before final assessments or feedback not returned in time for learning
Assessment design allows students to gain marks for work that is not their own

criteria.text: The provider must take reasonable steps to detect and prevent academic misconduct including but not limited to plagiarism and use of essay mills
criteria.threshold: Reasonable steps taken to detect and prevent academic misconduct
risk_indicators: Lack of plagiarism detection systems or processes
No policies or procedures for identifying essay mill usage
Inadequate staff training on academic misconduct detection
Assessments designed without consideration for preventing cheating

criteria.text: Assessment must be valid, testing what it was designed to assess across the full scope of the course
criteria.threshold: Assessments test intended learning outcomes comprehensively
risk_indicators: Assessments focusing only on material taught at end of long courses
Mismatch between learning outcomes and assessment methods
Limited coverage of course content in assessment design
Assessment methods inappropriate for testing intended skills

criteria.text: Assessment must be reliable, ensuring consistent marking standards for equivalent student achievement
criteria.threshold: Consistent marking standards applied across equivalent work
risk_indicators: Different marks awarded for equivalent achievement levels
Inconsistent marking between different assessors
Lack of moderation processes for assessment marking
Variation in standards between similar projects or dissertations

criteria.text: Qualifications must be credible with evidenced explanations for increases in degree classifications
criteria.threshold: Credible qualifications with justified classification patterns
