risk_indicators: Unexplained increases in first class or 2i degree proportions
Changes to degree classification algorithms without achievement evidence
Research degrees awarded for group contribution rather than individual knowledge
Lack of rigorous evaluation before changes to academic regulations

criteria.text: In order to successfully rely on the exception in B4.3(b), the nature of the evidence a provider would need to put forward would go beyond articulating potential legal concerns or matters to which it has had regard in its decision-making and would require compelling evidence and reasoning on matters of law.
criteria.threshold: Compelling evidence and reasoning on legal matters required
risk_indicators: Provider citing general legal concerns without specific compelling evidence
Vague references to legal considerations without detailed legal reasoning
Failure to provide substantial legal justification for academic regulation exceptions

criteria.text: As the exception in B4.3(b) only applies 'to the extent' that a provider can demonstrate a conflict with the Equality Act 2010, it would not be sufficient for a provider to put forward evidence and reasoning about its academic regulations in general terms; rather, a provider would need to address the particular aspects of its academic regulations which it is seeking to justify under the exception, and the particular courses and assessments to which these aspects relate.
criteria.threshold: Specific demonstration of Equality Act conflicts for particular regulations, courses and assessments
risk_indicators: Generalised claims about academic regulations without specific course references
Broad statements about Equality Act conflicts without targeting particular assessments
Inability to identify specific regulatory aspects requiring exception
Lack of course-specific justification for regulatory variations

criteria.text: including by ensuring that all of the work of an individual student can be identified from the records
criteria.threshold: All individual student work must be identifiable from maintained records
risk_indicators: Student assessment records cannot be linked to specific individuals
Assessment work lacks proper identification or attribution systems
Records do not allow for tracking of individual student performance
Assessment portfolios are incomplete or inadequately documented

criteria.text: students are assessed effectively
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate effective assessment practices for all students
risk_indicators: High failure rates or unusually low pass rates in assessments
Student complaints about unfair or unclear assessment processes
Lack of assessment standards documentation or inconsistent application
External examiner concerns about assessment quality or standards

criteria.text: each assessment is valid and reliable
criteria.threshold: Ensure all assessments accurately measure intended learning outcomes consistently
risk_indicators: Assessment methods that do not align with stated learning outcomes
Significant variations in marking between different assessors
Absence of assessment validation processes or quality assurance mechanisms
Student appeals or grade disputes indicating assessment reliability issues

criteria.text: academic regulations are designed to ensure that relevant awards are credible
criteria.threshold: Maintain academic regulations that uphold the credibility of awarded qualifications
risk_indicators: Academic regulations that are unclear, incomplete, or inconsistently applied
Awards granted that do not meet sector-recognized standards
Lack of periodic review or updating of academic regulations
External scrutiny raising concerns about award credibility

criteria.text: academic regulations are designed to ensure the effective assessment of technical proficiency in the English language in a manner which appropriately reflects the level and content of the applicable higher education course
criteria.threshold: Ensure English language assessment is appropriate to course level and content
risk_indicators: Students graduating with insufficient English proficiency for their qualification level
English language assessment requirements that are too lenient or inappropriate for course demands
Employer or professional body concerns about graduates' English language competency
Absence of English language proficiency monitoring throughout the course

criteria.text: relevant awards granted to students are credible at the point of being granted and when compared to those granted previously
criteria.threshold: Maintain consistency and credibility of awards both currently and historically
risk_indicators: Grade inflation or unexplained improvements in student outcomes over time
Awards that appear inconsistent with student performance or course rigor
External examiner concerns about standards drift or grade credibility
Significant discrepancies in award standards compared to similar institutions or previous years

conditions.notes: Document excerpt only contains section headers and page references. Full criteria text for B4 Assessment and awards condition is not provided in the source material.

conditions.title: Sector-recognised standards

criteria.text: The provider must ensure that any higher education courses that it provides are delivered and assessed using methods that enable every student enrolled on each of those courses to demonstrate the extent to which they have achieved the intended learning outcomes for the qualification for which they are registered.
criteria.threshold: All higher education courses delivered and assessed using methods enabling students to demonstrate achievement of intended learning outcomes
risk_indicators: Assessment methods that do not align with stated learning outcomes
Students unable to demonstrate required competencies despite completing course requirements
Inconsistent assessment practices across similar courses or campuses
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about assessment validity

criteria.text: This condition relates to the standards applied to higher education provided in any manner or form by, or on behalf of, a provider (including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider).
criteria.threshold: Apply appropriate standards to all higher education provision regardless of delivery mode
risk_indicators: Different standards applied to different modes of delivery (online vs face-to-face)
Lack of clear documentation showing how standards are applied across all provision
Standards not applied to provision delivered by partner organizations
Inconsistent quality assurance processes across different types of higher education courses

criteria.text: Without prejudice to the principles and requirements provided for by any other condition of registration and the scope of B5.1, the provider must ensure that, in respect of any relevant awards granted to students who complete a higher education course provided by, or on behalf of, the provider (whether or not the provider is the awarding body): a. any standards set appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards; and b. awards are only granted to students whose knowledge and skills appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards.
criteria.threshold: Ensure all awards reflect sector-recognised standards and students meet these standards before graduation
risk_indicators: Awards granted to students who have not demonstrated required knowledge and skills
Course standards that do not align with relevant sector-recognised frameworks
Absence of robust assessment processes to verify student achievement against sector standards
External examiner reports highlighting gaps between student achievement and sector expectations

criteria.text: "higher education course" is to be interpreted: in accordance with the Higher Education and Research Act 2017; and so as to include, for the avoidance of doubt: a course of study; a programme of research; any further education course that forms an integrated part of a higher education course; and any module that forms part of a higher education course, whether or not that module is delivered as an integrated part of the course.
criteria.threshold: Proper interpretation and classification of higher education courses in line with statutory definitions
risk_indicators: Courses incorrectly classified as higher education when they should be further education
Modules delivered in isolation being treated as standalone qualifications inappropriately
Integrated further education components not being properly recognized as part of higher education provision
Inconsistent application of course definitions across different programmes

criteria.text: "relevant award" means: a research award; a taught award; and/or any other type of award or qualification in respect of a higher education course, including an award of credit granted in respect of a module that may form part of a larger higher education course, whether or not granted pursuant to an authorisation given by or under the Higher Education and Research Act 2017, another Act of Parliament or Royal Charter.
criteria.threshold: Accurate identification and classification of all types of awards and qualifications
risk_indicators: Awards being granted that do not align with statutory definitions
Module credits being treated inconsistently with qualification framework requirements
Confusion between different types of awards (research vs taught vs other)
Awards being made without proper consideration of their legal basis

criteria.text: "research award" and "taught award" have the meanings given in section 42(3) of the Higher Education and Research Act 2017.
criteria.threshold: Consistent application of statutory definitions for research and taught awards
risk_indicators: Awards incorrectly categorized as research when they should be taught or vice versa
Hybrid programmes not properly classified according to their primary characteristics
Documentation failing to reflect the correct award type
Student records showing inconsistency in award classification

criteria.text: "sector-recognised standards" means the standards contained in the document of that title published by the OfS from time to time.
criteria.threshold: Recognition and application of current sector-recognised standards as published by OfS
risk_indicators: Use of outdated versions of sector-recognised standards
Failure to implement updates to standards in a timely manner
Inconsistent interpretation of what constitutes sector-recognised standards
Documentation referencing incorrect or superseded standards

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All forms of higher education provision must be included within scope
risk_indicators: Provider excludes certain courses from quality assurance processes claiming they fall outside condition scope
Different quality standards applied to online vs face-to-face delivery modes
Microcredentials or apprenticeships treated as exempt from standard quality requirements
International or partnership courses receive reduced oversight compared to domestic provision

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: All students and delivery arrangements must be covered regardless of location or partnership structure
risk_indicators: Different quality standards for international vs domestic students
Partnership arrangements lack clear quality oversight responsibilities
Non-UK delivery locations receive insufficient monitoring
Subcontracted provision operates without adequate quality controls

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider is responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it is the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider has any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding bodies must ensure compliance even when not involved in course delivery
risk_indicators: Awarding body fails to monitor quality of courses delivered by teaching partners
Insufficient oversight mechanisms for awards granted to students taught elsewhere
Awarding responsibilities treated as purely administrative without quality assurance
Poor communication between awarding and teaching bodies regarding quality standards

criteria.text: Where a provider is not the awarding body for a course, this condition applies to a course the provider itself delivers, or which is delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, this means for example, that a provider delivering, or allowing another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by Pearson is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Teaching providers must ensure compliance regardless of who awards the qualification
risk_indicators: Provider assumes quality responsibility lies entirely with external awarding body
Courses leading to non-OfS regulated awards receive reduced quality oversight
Professional body or commercial awarding body courses excluded from internal quality processes
Teaching provider lacks quality assurance processes for externally awarded courses

criteria.text: "Relevant award" includes, but is not limited to, the awards made by a provider in relation to an apprenticeship.
criteria.threshold: Provider must understand scope of relevant awards including apprenticeships
risk_indicators: Provider excludes apprenticeship awards from regulatory reporting
Provider demonstrates confusion about which awards are considered 'relevant'
Provider fails to apply condition requirements to apprenticeship programmes

criteria.text: A provider is expected to retain appropriate records of students' assessed work for such regulatory purposes for a period of five years after the end date of a course.
criteria.threshold: Maintain comprehensive records of student assessed work for five years post-course completion
risk_indicators: Provider cannot produce student work samples when requested by OfS
Provider has gaps in assessment records for completed courses
Provider destroys student work records before five-year retention period
Provider fails to maintain anonymised assessment records where possible

criteria.text: Where possible, a provider is expected to retain records of student assessments in an anonymised form by removing students' personal data from the records. However, in doing so, it should ensure that removal of students' personal data would not limit the OfS's ability to assess the provider's compliance.
criteria.threshold: Anonymise assessment records where possible without compromising regulatory assessment capability
risk_indicators: Provider over-anonymises records making compliance assessment impossible
Provider fails to anonymise records where it would be possible to do so
Provider cannot demonstrate consideration of anonymisation options
Provider's anonymisation process removes data essential for regulatory review

criteria.text: The registered provider must keep and maintain records that enable all of the work of an individual student to be identified from the records.
criteria.threshold: All student work must be identifiable from provider records
risk_indicators: Student work cannot be traced to individual students in provider records
Gaps or inconsistencies in student work documentation systems
Inability to produce complete records of student assessed work upon request
Evidence of anonymous or unattributed student work submissions

criteria.text: Where students' personal data cannot be anonymised, the provider must retain this data for five years after the end date of a course.
criteria.threshold: Personal data retained for minimum five years post-course completion
risk_indicators: Student personal data destroyed before five-year retention period expires
Inadequate data retention policies or procedures
Missing student records within the five-year retention window
Inability to access or retrieve student personal data when required

criteria.text: the standards to be set for a provider's courses appropriately reflect sector-recognised standards
criteria.threshold: Standards set for courses must appropriately reflect sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Course standards significantly below comparable sector benchmarks
Lack of reference to established sector frameworks in course design
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about academic standards
Graduate outcomes substantially below sector norms for similar courses

criteria.text: the awards to be granted for those courses will only be granted to students with knowledge and skills which also appropriately reflect sector-recognised standards
criteria.threshold: Awards granted only to students demonstrating knowledge and skills reflecting sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: High pass rates with low achievement of learning outcomes
Significant gaps between intended and actual graduate capabilities
Awards granted without evidence of sector-standard knowledge acquisition
Assessment practices that do not adequately test required competencies

criteria.text: condition B5 is likely to result in a judgement that initial
criteria.threshold: Meet requirements for initial judgement
risk_indicators: Incomplete or fragmented condition documentation
Unclear assessment criteria or thresholds
Missing regulatory compliance framework
Inadequate preparation for initial evaluation

criteria.text: any standards set appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards
criteria.threshold: Standards must appropriately reflect applicable sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Programme standards deviate significantly from sector-recognised benchmarks
External examiner reports highlight misalignment with sector standards
Professional body accreditation withdrawn or under review
Student outcomes inconsistent with sector-recognised competency requirements

criteria.text: awards are only granted to students whose knowledge and skills appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards
criteria.threshold: Awards only granted when student knowledge and skills meet applicable sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Graduates failing professional registration or qualification requirements
Employer feedback indicating graduate competencies below sector expectations
Assessment practices not aligned with sector-recognised standards
Awards granted despite students not meeting sector competency benchmarks

conditions.notes: This condition focuses on ensuring that higher education providers maintain appropriate standards in course delivery and assessment methods. The condition includes summary guidance and information on evidence gathering and enforcement procedures.

conditions.title: Teaching Excellence Framework participation

criteria.text: Teaching Excellence Framework participation
criteria.threshold: Participation in the Teaching Excellence Framework when eligible
risk_indicators: Failure to submit TEF application when eligible
Withdrawal from TEF assessment process
Non-engagement with TEF requirements and deadlines
Absence of TEF award or participation status

criteria.text: Teaching Excellence Framework participation
criteria.threshold: Must participate in the Teaching Excellence Framework as required
risk_indicators: Institution fails to submit required TEF application by deadline
Institution withdraws from or refuses to participate in TEF assessment process
Institution does not provide required evidence or data for TEF evaluation
Institution receives formal notice of non-participation from OfS

criteria.text: The provider must make a submission in the next TEF exercise by the submission deadline published by the OfS, provided the provider meets the eligibility requirements and has at least 500 undergraduate students on higher education courses and at least two TEF indicators based on a denominator of at least 500 students.
criteria.threshold: Submission made by published deadline for eligible providers meeting student thresholds
risk_indicators: Provider has exceeded 500 undergraduate students but has not initiated TEF submission preparations
Provider meets both headcount and indicator thresholds but has missed previous TEF submission deadlines
Provider shows declining engagement with OfS communications regarding TEF submission requirements
Provider lacks adequate data systems to track student numbers for TEF eligibility calculations

criteria.text: The provider must participate in the Teaching Excellence Ongoing Framework.
criteria.threshold: Active participation in the Teaching Excellence Ongoing Framework
risk_indicators: Failure to submit required data or documentation for TEF assessment cycles
Non-engagement with TEF review processes or deadlines
Absence from published TEF participant lists or ratings
OfS correspondence regarding non-participation in TEF requirements

conditions.notes: This condition relates to institutional participation in the Teaching Excellence Framework (TEF) assessment scheme. Page references indicate summary and guidance are provided on page 132, with initial conditions detailed on page 134.

conditions.title: Quality

criteria.text: Quality
criteria.threshold: Maintain quality standards across all provision
risk_indicators: Declining student satisfaction scores in quality-related metrics
Increasing complaints about teaching or learning resources
External examiner reports highlighting quality concerns
Staff turnover affecting continuity of quality provision

criteria.text: have credible plans that would enable the provider, if registered, to comply with conditions B1, B2 and B4 from the date of registration
criteria.threshold: Credible plans demonstrating ability to comply with conditions B1, B2 and B4 from registration date
risk_indicators: Plans lack specific timelines or milestones for achieving compliance with B1, B2 and B4
Unrealistic assumptions about resource availability or capability development
Absence of detailed implementation strategies for quality assurance requirements
Plans do not address known regulatory or operational challenges

criteria.text: have the capacity and resources necessary to deliver, in practice, those plans
criteria.threshold: Adequate capacity and resources to implement compliance plans in practice
risk_indicators: Insufficient staffing levels or expertise to execute planned activities
Financial projections that do not support planned compliance investments
Lack of appropriate physical or technological infrastructure
Over-reliance on external providers without adequate contractual arrangements

criteria.text: "capacity and resources" includes, but is not limited to: the financial resources of the provider; the number, expertise, and experience of the staff employed, and to be employed, by the provider; the physical and digital learning resources deployed, and to be deployed, by the provider and the provider's management and governance arrangements.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate adequate financial, staffing, physical, digital, and governance resources
risk_indicators: Financial statements showing declining reserves or cash flow problems
High staff turnover rates or difficulty recruiting qualified academic staff
Inadequate library, IT infrastructure, or learning facilities reported by students
Weak management structures or governance arrangements lacking oversight

criteria.text: "credible" includes, but is not limited to, evidence of the provider's past performance delivering higher education.
criteria.threshold: Provide credible evidence of capability to deliver higher education
risk_indicators: Limited or poor track record in delivering higher education programmes
Previous regulatory interventions or sanctions from education authorities
Student complaints or poor outcomes in previous educational provision
Lack of relevant qualifications or experience among senior leadership team

criteria.text: This condition applies to the courses that the provider plans to provide when it is registered.
criteria.threshold: All planned courses must fall within scope
risk_indicators: Provider offering courses without proper registration or approval
Unclear documentation of which courses are planned for provision
Courses being delivered that were not included in registration application

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All forms of higher education delivery must comply regardless of format, level, or recognition status
risk_indicators: Provider treating online or micro-credential courses as exempt from regulations
Different quality standards applied to different delivery modes
Apprenticeship or research courses operating outside standard oversight processes
Failure to apply consistent standards across all course types and delivery methods

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: All students and partnership arrangements must be covered regardless of location or delivery mechanism
risk_indicators: Partnership arrangements operating without proper oversight or quality assurance
International students or overseas delivery receiving different treatment
Subcontracted teaching or services not meeting same standards as direct provision
Unclear accountability lines in partnership arrangements

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider would be responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it would be the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider would have any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding bodies must ensure compliance even when only responsible for granting awards
risk_indicators: Award-granting providers not monitoring quality of courses they validate
Unclear quality assurance processes for awards granted to external students
Insufficient oversight of courses delivered by other providers but leading to own awards

criteria.text: Where a provider would not be the awarding body for a course, this condition applies to a course the provider itself would deliver, or which would be delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, this means for example, that a provider that would deliver, or allow another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by Pearson is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses. Similarly, a provider that would deliver, or allow another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by another higher education provider, whether that awarding provider is located in England or elsewhere, is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Delivering providers must ensure compliance regardless of who awards the qualification
risk_indicators: Providers assuming external awarding bodies absolve them of responsibility
Different quality standards applied based on awarding body identity
Insufficient due diligence on courses delivered for non-OfS registered awarding bodies
Lack of clear accountability for course quality when awarding body is external

criteria.text: In practice, these provisions may result in more than one registered provider being responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to the same course.
criteria.threshold: Multiple providers must each ensure full compliance when sharing responsibility for a course
risk_indicators: Confusion over responsibility between multiple providers involved in same course
Providers assuming other parties are handling compliance requirements
Gaps in oversight due to unclear division of responsibilities
Lack of coordination between multiple responsible providers

criteria.text: The OfS is likely to draw on evidence submitted by a provider as part of its application for registration to make a judgement about whether the provider has "credible" plans in accordance with this requirement. For example, evidence submitted about the policies and processes a provider proposes to have in place to ensure compliance with ongoing conditions B1, B2 and B4, and evidence of how the provider intends to assess its own compliance with those conditions, is likely to be relevant.
criteria.threshold: Provider must demonstrate credible plans for compliance with ongoing conditions B1, B2 and B4
risk_indicators: Vague or incomplete policies and processes for regulatory compliance
Lack of self-assessment mechanisms for ongoing condition compliance
Insufficient detail in implementation plans for regulatory requirements
No clear timeline or milestones for establishing compliance frameworks

criteria.text: Where a provider seeking registration has previously delivered, or is currently delivering, higher education courses, the "plans" required under this condition may include evidence relating to the provider's experience of delivery of those courses. The OfS will have regard to that evidence in determining whether the condition is satisfied.
criteria.threshold: Provider must provide evidence of relevant experience in higher education delivery where applicable
risk_indicators: Poor track record in previous higher education course delivery
Unresolved issues from past course delivery that could impact future compliance
Lack of demonstrable learning from previous delivery experience
Insufficient evidence of quality improvement over time in course delivery

criteria.text: Where a registered provider is seeking registration in a different category of registration, this requires the OfS to deregister the provider and make a new registration decision by deciding whether each of the initial conditions is satisfied. In these circumstances "date of registration" means the date of the new registration.
criteria.threshold: Provider must satisfy all initial conditions afresh when changing registration category
risk_indicators: Failure to meet updated requirements for the new registration category
Insufficient preparation for enhanced obligations of higher registration category
Gaps in capability between current and desired registration category
Inadequate transition planning for category change requirements

criteria.text: The OfS is likely to draw on evidence submitted by a provider as part of its application for registration to make a judgement about whether the provider has the "capacity and resources" necessary to deliver, in practice, its plans.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate capacity and resources to deliver plans in practice through registration evidence
risk_indicators: Inadequate financial resources documented in application materials
Management and governance arrangements that appear insufficient for planned scale of operations
Evidence of resource constraints affecting delivery capacity
Disconnect between ambitious plans and available resources

criteria.text: The OfS will assess compliance with this initial condition for all providers seeking registration. It may commission the designated quality body to conduct an assessment of quality to provide information to the OfS to inform the OfS's decision about whether the condition is satisfied.
criteria.threshold: Pass OfS assessment of compliance, potentially involving designated quality body evaluation
risk_indicators: Failure to provide specified information to designated quality body
Poor performance during site visits or staff/student interviews
Inability to demonstrate quality arrangements during assessment
Assessment findings indicating inadequate quality systems

criteria.text: Where a provider, or another legal entity that the OfS considers to be operating substantially the same higher education business, has previously been registered, a history of non-compliance with ongoing conditions B1, B2 or B4 is likely to result in a judgement that initial condition is not satisfied.
criteria.threshold: Maintain clean compliance history with conditions B1, B2 or B4 if previously registered
risk_indicators: Previous regulatory action for non-compliance with conditions B1, B2 or B4
History of quality or standards failures at related legal entities
Pattern of recurring compliance issues in previous registration
Unresolved enforcement actions from previous registration period

criteria.text: have credible plans that would enable the provider, if registered, to comply with Initial conditions B1, B2 and B4 from the date of registration
criteria.threshold: Credible plans demonstrating ability to comply with conditions B1, B2 and B4 from registration date
risk_indicators: Plans lack detail on how compliance with B1, B2, B4 will be achieved
Unrealistic timelines or milestones in compliance plans
Plans do not address specific requirements of conditions B1, B2, B4
Evidence supporting plan credibility is weak or absent

criteria.text: have the capacity and resources necessary to deliver, in practice, those plans
criteria.threshold: Adequate capacity and resources to practically implement compliance plans
risk_indicators: Insufficient financial resources allocated to implement plans
Lack of qualified staff or expertise to deliver planned activities
Infrastructure or systems inadequate to support plan delivery
No clear resource allocation or budget for compliance activities

conditions.notes: This condition appears to be a section header only. Full criterion text would typically be found in the detailed guidance sections referenced (pages 134-136).

conditions.title: Standards

criteria.text: The registered higher education provider must ensure that academic standards are credible and rigorous and are comparable with those delivered by other registered higher education providers which deliver courses of a similar level and subject matter.
criteria.threshold: Academic standards must be credible, rigorous and comparable with similar providers
risk_indicators: Grade inflation patterns significantly exceeding sector norms
External examiner reports raising concerns about standard comparability
Assessment practices lacking rigor or appropriate challenge
Progression and completion rates dramatically higher than comparable providers without clear pedagogical justification

criteria.text: This condition relates to the standards to be applied to higher education to be provided in any manner or form by, or on behalf of, a provider (including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider would be responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider).
criteria.threshold: Condition applies to all forms of higher education provision by or on behalf of the provider
risk_indicators: Provider unclear about which programmes or partnerships the condition covers
Standards applied inconsistently across different modes of delivery
Confusion about responsibility for standards in partnership arrangements

criteria.text: Without prejudice to the principles and requirements provided for by any other condition of registration and the scope of B8.1, the provider must demonstrate, in a credible manner, that any standards to be set and/or applied in respect of any relevant awards granted to students who complete a higher education course provided by, or on behalf of, the provider (if registered), whether or not the provider is the awarding body, appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards.
criteria.threshold: Credible demonstration that award standards appropriately reflect applicable sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Awards not aligned with relevant Subject Benchmark Statements or Framework for Higher Education Qualifications
Lack of credible evidence showing how standards are set and maintained
Standards documentation does not reference or map to sector-recognised frameworks
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about standard-setting processes

criteria.text: "credible" includes, but is not limited to, evidence of the provider's past performance delivering higher education.
criteria.threshold: Provider must demonstrate credibility through past performance in higher education delivery
risk_indicators: Limited or no documented history of delivering higher education courses
Poor performance metrics in previous higher education delivery
Lack of evidence of successful completion rates in past courses
Absence of testimonials or references from previous students or partners

criteria.text: "higher education course" is to be interpreted: i. in accordance with the Higher Education and Research Act 2017; and ii. so as to include, for the avoidance of doubt: A. a course of study; B. a programme of research; C. any further education course that forms an integrated part of a higher education course; and D. any module that forms part of a higher education course, whether or not that module is delivered as an integrated part of the course.
criteria.threshold: Provider must correctly interpret and apply the definition of higher education course as specified
risk_indicators: Misclassification of courses as higher education when they do not meet HERA 2017 criteria
Failure to include integrated further education components in higher education course definitions
Incorrect treatment of standalone modules that form part of larger courses
Inconsistent application of course definitions across different programmes

criteria.text: "relevant award" means: i. a research award; ii. a taught award; and/or iii. any other type of award or qualification in respect of a higher education course, including an award of credit granted in respect of a module that may form part of a larger higher education course, whether or not granted pursuant to an authorisation given by or under the Higher Education and Research Act 2017, another Act of Parliament or Royal Charter.
criteria.threshold: Provider must correctly identify and categorise all types of relevant awards
risk_indicators: Awards or qualifications not properly classified according to research, taught, or other categories
Credit awards for modules not recognised as relevant awards
Confusion about which awards require specific authorisation
Inconsistent treatment of awards across different statutory frameworks

criteria.text: "research award" and "taught award" have the meanings given in section 42(3) of the Higher Education and Research Act 2017.
criteria.threshold: Provider must apply the statutory definitions of research and taught awards from HERA 2017
risk_indicators: Use of non-statutory definitions for research or taught awards
Misclassification of awards between research and taught categories
Failure to reference or apply section 42(3) of HERA 2017
Inconsistent categorisation of awards that could fall into either category

criteria.text: "sector-recognised standards" means the standards contained in the document of that title published by the OfS from time to time, and which apply as at the date of the provider's application for registration.
criteria.threshold: Provider must apply the current version of sector-recognised standards as published by OfS at time of application
risk_indicators: Use of outdated versions of sector-recognised standards
Reference to standards not published by OfS
Application of standards that were not current at the date of registration application
Failure to update standards when OfS publishes revised versions

criteria.text: This condition applies to the courses that the provider plans to provide when it is registered.
criteria.threshold: All planned courses must fall within scope of regulatory requirements
risk_indicators: Provider launches courses without proper registration or notification
Confusion about which courses are included in registration scope
Courses offered that were not declared in registration application

criteria.text: The reference to higher education provided "in any manner or form" includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to microcredentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or a combination of delivery approaches.
criteria.threshold: All forms of higher education delivery must comply regardless of format, level, or recognition status
risk_indicators: Provider treats some course types as exempt from regulatory requirements
Different quality standards applied to online vs face-to-face delivery
Microcredentials or short courses delivered without proper oversight

criteria.text: This condition applies to any higher education provided "by, or on behalf of, a provider". This includes higher education provided to all of the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf). This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: All provision delivered directly or through partnerships must comply, regardless of student location
risk_indicators: Partner organizations delivering courses without proper oversight
International provision treated as outside regulatory scope
Inconsistent standards between direct and partnership delivery

criteria.text: The reference to "including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider would be responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider" means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions of this condition where it would be the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider would have any other role in the design or delivery of that course.
criteria.threshold: Awarding body responsibility triggers full compliance requirements even without course delivery involvement
risk_indicators: Awarding body lacks oversight of courses for which it grants awards
Quality assurance gaps between teaching provider and awarding body
Awards granted without proper validation of learning outcomes

criteria.text: This requirement is designed to assess a provider's ability to comply with ongoing
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate ability to comply with ongoing requirements
risk_indicators: Incomplete or fragmented compliance documentation
Lack of clear ongoing compliance framework or processes
History of non-compliance with regulatory requirements
Absence of dedicated compliance monitoring systems

criteria.text: The provider must demonstrate that the standards set in courses they will deliver when registered appropriately reflect sector-recognised standards, as assessed through a designated quality body standards assessment.
criteria.threshold: Standards assessment by designated quality body confirms courses meet sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Course standards fail to align with sector-recognised benchmarks during assessment
Designated quality body identifies significant gaps in academic standards
Provider unable to demonstrate appropriate standard-setting processes
Assessment reveals inconsistent or inadequate quality assurance mechanisms

criteria.text: condition B8 is satisfied
criteria.threshold: Must satisfy condition B8 requirements
risk_indicators: Previous history of non-compliance with ongoing conditions
Failure to provide adequate information to designated quality body
OfS assessment indicates condition requirements not met
Legal entity with substantially same business shows compliance issues

criteria.text: condition B8 is not satisfied
criteria.threshold: Must satisfy condition B8 requirements
risk_indicators: Academic standards falling below sector-recognised standards
External examiner reports highlighting concerns about standards
Student achievement rates significantly below benchmark
Quality assurance processes failing to maintain standards

criteria.text: The provider must demonstrate that any standards to be set and/or applied in respect of any relevant awards granted to students who complete a higher education course provided by, or on behalf of, the provider (if registered), whether or not the provider is the awarding body, appropriately reflect any applicable sector-recognised standards.
criteria.threshold: Standards for awards must appropriately reflect sector-recognised standards
risk_indicators: Awards fail to meet subject benchmark statements or other sector-recognised standards
External examiner reports identify standards below sector expectations
Graduate outcomes significantly below sector norms for comparable programmes
Lack of evidence demonstrating alignment with relevant professional body requirements

conditions.notes: This condition focuses on ensuring academic standards maintain credibility and rigor while being comparable across the sector for similar level courses and subject areas.

conditions.title: Guidance on consumer protection law

criteria.text: The provider must comply with all applicable consumer protection law.
criteria.threshold: Full compliance with consumer protection law requirements
risk_indicators: Student complaints alleging misleading course information or marketing materials
Failure to provide clear terms and conditions or refund policies
Regulatory investigations or enforcement actions by trading standards or competition authorities
Non-compliance with requirements for transparent pricing and contract terms

criteria.text: The registered provider must have regard to relevant guidance about how to comply with consumer protection law.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate awareness of and consideration given to consumer protection law guidance
risk_indicators: No evidence of reviewing or considering consumer protection guidance
Student complaints citing misleading information or unfair contract terms
Marketing materials that make unsubstantiated claims about courses or outcomes
Lack of clear refund policies or terms and conditions for students

criteria.text: The provider must demonstrate that in developing and implementing its policies, procedures and terms and conditions it has given due regard to relevant guidance about how to comply with consumer protection law.
criteria.threshold: Due regard given to relevant guidance when developing and implementing policies, procedures and terms and conditions for consumer protection law compliance
risk_indicators: Provider fails to follow procedures set out in original submission as evidenced through third party feedback from students, CMA, OIA or Trading Standards
Judicial proceedings or regulatory action by Trading Standards, OIA, CMA indicating non-compliance with statutory obligations
Student complaints to OIA alleging provider is not operating in compliance with consumer protection law
Inadequate management capacity to ensure ongoing compliance with consumer protection obligations

criteria.text: The provider must demonstrate that in developing and implementing its policies, procedures and terms and conditions, it has given due regard to relevant guidance about how to comply with consumer protection law.
criteria.threshold: Evidence that relevant consumer protection guidance has been considered in policy development and implementation
risk_indicators: Policies and procedures developed without reference to consumer protection guidance
Terms and conditions that contain unfair or misleading provisions
No documented evidence of consideration of consumer protection requirements
Student complaints relating to unfair contractual terms or misleading information

conditions.notes: This condition requires providers to meet their obligations under consumer protection law when dealing with students as consumers. Page references indicate detailed guidance, assessment criteria and expected behaviours are provided in the full document.

conditions.title: Student complaints scheme

criteria.text: The provider must have in place a student complaints scheme that enables the effective handling of student academic and non-academic complaints
criteria.threshold: Effective student complaints scheme covering academic and non-academic matters
risk_indicators: High volume of unresolved student complaints
Absence of clear complaints procedures or timelines
Student dissatisfaction with complaints handling process
Lack of independent review stage in complaints process

criteria.text: The provider must have in place a student complaints scheme which enables the fair, transparent and timely handling of student complaints
criteria.threshold: Fair, transparent and timely student complaints handling system
risk_indicators: Complaints not being resolved within published timescales
Students reporting dissatisfaction with complaints process outcomes
Lack of clear published complaints procedures or timescales
High volume of complaints escalated to external bodies

criteria.text: Cooperate with the requirements of the student complaints scheme run by the Office of the Independent Adjudicator for Higher Education, including the subscription requirements.
criteria.threshold: Full cooperation with OIA scheme requirements and maintaining subscription
risk_indicators: Outstanding subscription fees or unpaid charges to the OIA
Failure to respond to OIA requests within specified timeframes
Non-compliance with OIA recommendations or decisions
Lack of cooperation during OIA investigations

criteria.text: Make students aware of their ability to use the scheme.
criteria.threshold: Students are informed of their right to access the OIA complaints scheme
risk_indicators: Absence of OIA information in student handbooks or websites
Students unaware of OIA scheme during complaint processes
No clear signposting to OIA at conclusion of internal complaints procedures
Missing or inadequate OIA awareness materials in student communications

criteria.text: The provider must cooperate with the requirements of the student complaints scheme run by the OIA, including information that the OfS receives from the OIA in relation to a registered provider's cooperation with the complaints handling scheme.
criteria.threshold: Active cooperation with OIA complaints handling requirements
risk_indicators: OIA reports non-cooperation or delayed responses to complaints procedures
Provider fails to implement OIA recommendations within specified timeframes
Multiple complaints to OIA about provider's handling of student complaints
Provider disputes or challenges OIA jurisdiction inappropriately

criteria.text: The provider must cooperate with the requirements of the student complaints scheme run by the OIA, including information that the OfS receives from the OIA in relation to the payment of the OIA's fee.
criteria.threshold: Timely payment of OIA fees as required
risk_indicators: Late or non-payment of OIA subscription fees
OIA reports payment arrears or disputes
Provider questions or refuses to pay required OIA fees
Financial difficulties affecting statutory payment obligations

criteria.text: Making students aware of their ability to use the student complaints scheme
criteria.threshold: Provider must actively inform students about their ability to use the OIA student complaints scheme through published information and contractual/marketing materials
risk_indicators: Student complaints scheme information is absent or difficult to find on the provider's website
Marketing and admissions materials contain no reference to student complaints procedures
Contractual documentation fails to mention the OIA scheme or student complaint rights
Students demonstrate lack of awareness of complaint mechanisms during surveys or interactions

criteria.text: Co-operate with the requirements of the student complaints scheme run by the Office of the Independent Adjudicator for Higher Education, including the subscription requirements.
criteria.threshold: Full cooperation with OIA requirements and maintained subscription
risk_indicators: Outstanding subscription fees or payments to the OIA
Failure to respond to OIA requests within required timeframes
Non-compliance with OIA recommendations or decisions
Absence of internal processes for handling OIA correspondence

criteria.text: Make students aware of their ability to use the scheme.
criteria.threshold: Students are informed about their right to use the OIA scheme
risk_indicators: No reference to OIA scheme in student handbooks or websites
Student surveys showing lack of awareness of complaints procedures
Absence of OIA information in final response letters to student complaints
Missing or outdated OIA contact details in student-facing materials

conditions.notes: This condition requires providers to maintain an effective system for handling both academic and non-academic student complaints, ensuring students have appropriate recourse when issues arise.

conditions.title: Student protection plan

criteria.text: The registered higher education provider must have a student protection plan that is appropriate to the provider and has been approved by the OfS.
criteria.threshold: Approved student protection plan appropriate to the provider
risk_indicators: Student protection plan has not been submitted to OfS for approval
Plan does not cover key risks relevant to the provider's context
Plan lacks detail on refund arrangements and alternative study options
No evidence of regular review and updating of the protection plan

criteria.text: The provider must have a student protection plan which has been approved by the OfS.
criteria.threshold: Approved student protection plan in place covering all required scenarios
risk_indicators: No current student protection plan approved by OfS
Student protection plan approval has expired or been withdrawn
Significant changes to provision not reflected in current plan
Plan does not cover all student cohorts or programmes offered

criteria.text: Have in force and publish a student protection plan which has been approved by the OfS as appropriate for its assessment of the regulatory risk presented by the provider and for the risk to continuation of study of all of its students.
criteria.threshold: Must have an OfS-approved student protection plan published and in force
risk_indicators: Student protection plan has not been updated or reviewed within required timeframes
Published plan does not align with OfS approval conditions or regulatory risk assessment
Plan fails to address continuation risks for all student cohorts
No evidence of plan accessibility or publication to students

criteria.text: Take all reasonable steps to implement the provisions of the plan if the events set out in the plan take place.
criteria.threshold: Must actively implement plan provisions when triggering events occur
risk_indicators: Failure to activate plan provisions when qualifying events occur
Inadequate resources allocated to implement plan measures
Students report receiving no support during events requiring plan implementation
Implementation efforts are incomplete or significantly delayed

criteria.text: Inform the OfS of events, except for the closure of an individual course, that require the implementation of the provisions of the plan.
criteria.threshold: Must notify OfS of all relevant triggering events except individual course closures
risk_indicators: Delayed or missing notifications to OfS about triggering events
Events requiring plan implementation are occurring but not reported
Pattern of poor communication with OfS during operational disruptions
Uncertainty about which events require OfS notification

criteria.text: To register, a provider is required to produce a student protection plan which meets the requirements set out below. The purpose of a student protection plan is to preserve the continuation and quality of study for all of the provider's students whenever a risk to the continued study of students crystallises.
criteria.threshold: Must produce a compliant student protection plan that preserves continuation and quality of study
risk_indicators: No student protection plan available or plan significantly out of date
Student protection plan lacks provider-specific risk assessment
Plan fails to address key operational risks to student continuation
Students report lack of awareness of protection measures during disruptions

criteria.text: The student protection plan must be tailored to the specific circumstances of an individual provider. It must include the provider's assessment of the risks to the continuation of study of the provider's students, the likelihood that those risks will crystallise, and the severity of the impact on students should the risks crystallise.
criteria.threshold: Plan must be provider-specific with comprehensive risk assessment including likelihood and impact analysis
risk_indicators: Generic or template-based plan not tailored to provider circumstances
Risk assessment lacks analysis of likelihood or severity of impacts
Key provider-specific risks omitted from assessment
Risk analysis appears superficial or inadequately evidenced

criteria.text: On the basis of the provider's risk assessment, the plan must set out the measures that the provider has put in place to mitigate those risks that it considers to be reasonably likely to crystallise. This will include existing procedures that are in place to respond should risks crystallise, and the steps the provider will take to ensure that mitigations are fair and reasonable for students.
criteria.threshold: Must detail specific mitigation measures for likely risks with fair and reasonable student protections
risk_indicators: Mitigation measures are vague, generic or lack implementation details
No clear procedures for responding when risks crystallise
Mitigation measures appear inadequate for identified high-likelihood risks
No evidence of consideration of fairness or reasonableness to students

criteria.text: The plan should also contain information about the provider's refund and compensation policy for cases where it is not possible to preserve continuation of study.
criteria.threshold: Must include clear refund and compensation policy for when continuation cannot be preserved
risk_indicators: No refund or compensation policy included in plan
Refund policy is unclear or appears unfair to students
Policy lacks detail on eligibility criteria or calculation methods
Students report confusion about entitlements during course disruptions

criteria.text: The plan should be revised regularly to ensure that the risk assessment remains current and the mitigating measures remain practicable, relevant and effective. The plan should be produced in collaboration with students to ensure that their views, interests and needs are taken into account. The plan should be published in a clear and accessible way.
criteria.threshold: Plan must be regularly updated, developed with student input, and published accessibly
risk_indicators: Plan has not been updated for extended periods despite changing circumstances
No evidence of student involvement in plan development or review
Plan is not publicly available or published in inaccessible format
Student representatives report no consultation on protection planning

criteria.text: A provider will satisfy this condition by informing the OfS promptly of events that require the implementation of any of the provisions of the plan.
criteria.threshold: Prompt notification to OfS of events requiring plan implementation
risk_indicators: Delays in reporting triggering events to OfS
Failure to identify when plan provisions should be implemented
Inadequate incident reporting systems or procedures
Evidence of events occurring without corresponding OfS notification

criteria.text: A provider will satisfy this condition by implementing the provisions of the plan when the events set out in the plan take place.
criteria.threshold: Full implementation of student protection plan provisions when triggering events occur
risk_indicators: Student complaints to OIA or elsewhere that plan provisions have not been implemented as stated
Provider unable to demonstrate how it intends to implement its plan when required
Failure to meet obligations explicitly set out in the student protection plan
Evidence that plan measures are not being triggered when specified events occur

criteria.text: Have in force and publish a student protection plan which has been approved by the OfS as appropriate for its assessment of the regulatory risk presented by the provider and for the risk to continuation of study of all of its students.
criteria.threshold: Approved and published student protection plan appropriate for regulatory risk and continuation of study risks
risk_indicators: Student protection plan has not been updated or approved by OfS within required timeframes
Plan is not publicly accessible or lacks required detail on student protections
Plan does not adequately address identified risks to student continuation
Provider has high regulatory risk profile but plan does not reflect this appropriately

criteria.text: Take all reasonable steps to implement the provisions of the plan if the events set out in the plan take place.
criteria.threshold: Full implementation of student protection plan provisions when triggering events occur
risk_indicators: Failure to activate plan provisions despite occurrence of triggering events
Inadequate or delayed implementation of protective measures for affected students
Students report lack of support or information during plan implementation
Provider cannot demonstrate reasonable steps were taken to implement plan provisions

criteria.text: Inform the OfS of events, except for the closure of an individual course, that require the implementation of the provisions of the plan.
criteria.threshold: Timely notification to OfS of all plan-triggering events except individual course closures
risk_indicators: OfS discovers triggering events through sources other than provider notification
Significant delays in reporting events that require plan implementation
Provider fails to recognize when events trigger student protection plan provisions
Pattern of late or incomplete reporting of material changes affecting students

conditions.notes: This condition requires providers to maintain an OfS-approved student protection plan that is tailored to their specific circumstances and risks. The plan must outline arrangements to protect students' continuation of study and academic interests in the event of provider closure or course discontinuation.

conditions.title: Student protection directions

criteria.text: Student protection directions
criteria.threshold: Compliance with student protection directions issued by the OfS
risk_indicators: Failure to implement required student protection measures within specified timeframes
Non-compliance with specific directions issued by the OfS regarding student protection
Inadequate student protection plans that do not meet regulatory requirements
Evidence of students being harmed due to non-implementation of protection directions

criteria.text: The registered higher education provider must comply with any student protection direction given by the OfS under section 15 of HERA.
criteria.threshold: Full compliance with all OfS student protection directions issued under HERA section 15
risk_indicators: Failure to implement measures specified in OfS student protection directions within required timeframes
Evidence of non-compliance with specific requirements outlined in issued directions
Lack of documented systems or processes to monitor and ensure adherence to protection directions
Student complaints or concerns indicating that protection measures mandated by OfS are not being followed

criteria.text: The provider must comply with any Student Protection Direction in circumstances where the OfS reasonably considers that there is a material risk that the provider will, or will be required by the operation of law to, fully or substantially cease the provision of higher education in England ("Market Exit Risk").
criteria.threshold: Full compliance with all Student Protection Directions issued by OfS during Market Exit Risk
risk_indicators: Financial distress indicators suggesting potential market exit
Failure to respond to or acknowledge Student Protection Direction within specified timescales
Non-submission of required Market Exit Plan when directed
Evidence of closure preparations without adequate student protection measures

criteria.text: A Student Protection Direction may be varied or revoked (wholly or in part) by express provision in a subsequent Student Protection Direction issued by the OfS in accordance with this condition of registration, and the OfS may otherwise revoke a Student Protection Direction by issuing a notice in writing to the provider.
criteria.threshold: Acknowledgement and compliance with variations or revocations of Student Protection Directions
risk_indicators: Continued implementation of revoked or superseded directions
Failure to acknowledge receipt of variation or revocation notices
Confusion about which version of directions are currently in effect

criteria.text: A Student Protection Direction (or, as the case may be, part of a Student Protection Direction) will cease to have effect in accordance with the following provisions: a. in circumstances where a Student Protection Direction is varied or revoked (wholly or in part) by a subsequent Student Protection Direction, on and from the time and date that the subsequent Student Protection Direction takes effect; or b. in circumstances where a Student Protection Direction is revoked by a notice in writing, on and from the time and date specified in that notice in writing.
criteria.threshold: Understanding and compliance with cessation timelines for Student Protection Directions
risk_indicators: Continued implementation beyond specified cessation dates
Lack of systems to track direction timelines and cessation dates
Failure to implement subsequent directions that supersede previous ones

criteria.text: Where a Student Protection Direction ceases to have effect at any time (for any reason), that cessation does not in any way affect the ability of the OfS to investigate and/or take any form of regulatory or enforcement action in respect of any non-compliance with that Student Protection Direction (whether or not the non-compliance remains ongoing in nature) which took place during the period that the Student Protection Direction was in effect.
criteria.threshold: Recognition that past non-compliance remains subject to regulatory action despite direction cessation
risk_indicators: Documented evidence of non-compliance during active direction periods
Failure to maintain records of compliance activities during direction periods
Assumption that cessation of direction eliminates accountability for past failures

criteria.text: where a provider cannot demonstrate that it is likely to have access to sufficient funds (for example, operating cash funds, investments, or funding that can be released from surplus assets or obtained from other sources) to meet its day-to-day costs, and any other liabilities due, within the next 12 months, including where a provider's ability to meet its day-to-day costs or liabilities is likely to be reliant on specific factors and the OfS judges that there is material uncertainty about whether these will be delivered in practice. These specific factors might include, but are not limited to: i. Securing additional borrowing or investment; ii. Delivering significant business restructuring or other cost saving measures; iii. The decision or actions of a third party.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate likely access to sufficient funds to meet day-to-day costs and liabilities within next 12 months without material uncertainty
risk_indicators: Provider cannot demonstrate 12-month funding pipeline or cash flow projections
Dependence on uncertain third-party decisions or actions for operational funding
Reliance on unconfirmed borrowing or investment to meet immediate liabilities
Implementation of emergency cost-cutting measures or business restructuring with uncertain outcomes

criteria.text: 'Student protection plan' means a document or documents approved by the OfS under initial and general ongoing
criteria.threshold: Maintain OfS-approved student protection plan documentation
risk_indicators: Student protection plan has expired or lacks current OfS approval
Plan does not cover key operational risks or student continuation scenarios
No evidence of plan updates following material changes to provision
Student protection arrangements are inadequately communicated to students

criteria.text: The provider must comply with any Student Protection Direction in circumstances where the OfS reasonably considers that there is a material risk that the provider will, or will be required by the operation of law to, fully or substantially cease the provision of higher education in England ('Market Exit Risk').
criteria.threshold: Full compliance with Student Protection Direction when facing Market Exit Risk
risk_indicators: Provider fails to implement required student protection measures when directed by OfS
Provider does not have adequate plans in place for student transfer or course completion during market exit scenarios
Provider ignores or delays response to OfS Student Protection Direction
Provider continues normal operations without addressing student protection requirements despite identified market exit risk

conditions.notes: This condition relates to compliance with student protection directions issued by the Office for Students to safeguard student interests.

conditions.title: Public interest governance

criteria.text: Public interest governance
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate governance arrangements that serve the public interest
risk_indicators: Board decisions prioritize private interests over public benefit
Lack of transparency in governance processes and decision-making
Absence of independent oversight or external accountability measures
Conflicts of interest not properly managed or disclosed

criteria.text: The governing body of the provider must be constituted so as to ensure that it includes persons of experience and expertise in matters relevant to the provider, that it has appropriate independence to enable the effective discharge of its functions and that it is not dominated by the interests of any single group.
criteria.threshold: Governing body composition with relevant expertise, independence, and balanced representation without single group dominance
risk_indicators: Governing body dominated by single stakeholder group or narrow interests
Lack of diversity in professional backgrounds or expertise among governing body members
Evidence of conflicts of interest not being properly managed or declared
Governing body members lacking experience in higher education, business, or relevant professional fields

criteria.text: The governing body of the provider must conduct itself in accordance with the principles of public life.
criteria.threshold: Adherence to the seven principles of public life: selflessness, integrity, objectivity, accountability, openness, honesty, and leadership
risk_indicators: Governing body decisions showing evidence of personal gain or private interest
Lack of transparency in governing body proceedings and decision-making
Failure to publish required governance information or meeting minutes
Evidence of governing body members not declaring interests or acting without integrity

criteria.text: The provider's governing documents must uphold the public interest governance principles that are applicable to the provider
criteria.threshold: Governing documents must reflect and provide framework for public interest governance principles as identified in Annex B
risk_indicators: Provider makes changes to governing documents affecting public interest governance principles without notifying OfS
Governing documents lack provisions for key governance principles such as conflict of interest management or academic freedom
Provider's self-assessment shows gaps in how governing documents uphold required principles
Governing documents do not provide suitable framework to identify, define, and demonstrate compliance with active governance requirements

criteria.text: The provider operates in a way that is consistent with its governing documents, including that the provider seeks to achieve its objectives, that its decisions are informed by its values, and that decisions are in fact taken by the body or individual identified in the governing documents as taking those decisions, acting without direction coercion or covert influence.
criteria.threshold: Management and governance arrangements must be adequate and effective for the provider's size, complexity and risk environment
risk_indicators: Decisions being made by bodies or individuals not identified in governing documents
Evidence of external coercion or covert influence in decision-making processes
Actions and decisions that contradict stated objectives and values
Failure to follow processes and structures set out in governing documents

criteria.text: The provider has in place adequate and effective management and governance arrangements that operate in accordance with its governing documents.
criteria.threshold: Governance arrangements must be demonstrably adequate and effective through records of decision-making, monitoring, and outcomes
risk_indicators: Poor or missing records of decision-making processes (agendas, reports, minutes)
Failure to monitor implementation of decisions effectively
Courses not being delivered as advertised
Repeated breaches of conditions of registration without appropriate mitigation

criteria.text: The provider has in place adequate and effective management and governance arrangements to deliver, in practice, the public interest governance principles that are applicable to it.
criteria.threshold: Actions must demonstrably deliver applicable public interest governance principles including transparency, accountability, and statutory compliance
risk_indicators: Lack of student representation on governing body where legally possible
Failure to publish clear value for money statements or senior staff pay information
Non-compliance with freedom of speech statutory duties
Absence of written commitment to higher education remuneration code without explanation

criteria.text: The provider's governing documents must uphold the Initial and ongoing public interest governance principles that are applicable to the provider.
criteria.threshold: Governing documents must incorporate and uphold applicable public interest governance principles
risk_indicators: Governing documents do not reference or incorporate required public interest governance principles
Conflicts between governing documents and public interest governance requirements
Failure to update governing documents when new public interest governance principles become applicable
Governance decisions that contradict established public interest principles

conditions.notes: Document references Summary, Guidance, Assessment, and Behaviours sections but detailed criterion text not provided in the excerpt.

conditions.title: Management and governance

criteria.text: The provider must be well managed and governed.
criteria.threshold: Effective management and governance structures and processes
risk_indicators: Board or governing body meetings cancelled or postponed frequently
Key management positions vacant for extended periods
Decisions made without proper authority or governance approval
Lack of clear accountability structures or reporting lines

criteria.text: Management and governance
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate effective management and governance systems are in place
risk_indicators: Board meetings cancelled or poorly attended by senior leadership
Key governance positions vacant for extended periods
Repeated delays in producing required statutory reports or regulatory returns
Evidence of conflicts of interest not being properly managed or declared

criteria.text: Operate in accordance with its governing documents.
criteria.threshold: Full compliance with all governing documents in operational practice
risk_indicators: Board decisions that contradict constitutional provisions or articles of association
Operating procedures that deviate from stated governance frameworks
Failure to follow prescribed decision-making processes outlined in governing documents
Non-compliance with statutory obligations specified in constitutional documents

criteria.text: Deliver, in practice, the public interest governance principles that are applicable to it.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrated implementation of applicable public interest governance principles in operational practice
risk_indicators: Lack of transparency in decision-making processes
Absence of effective accountability mechanisms to stakeholders
Poor or inconsistent stakeholder engagement practices
Evidence of decisions prioritizing private over public interest
